The community's central PV location places it equidistant from the Scottsdale and Phoenix sides of the Valley, with AJ's Fine Foods, The Borgata, and Lincoln Drive's restaurant row all within a few minutes. The terrain is gently elevated desert with native vegetation — saguaro, ironwood, and palo verde — preserved throughout the community. Homes are set on generous lots behind guard-gated entry, with architectural guidelines emphasizing contemporary desert design: clean lines, natural materials, and floor-to-ceiling glass that blurs the boundary between indoor and outdoor living.

Azure attracts a buyer who values design as much as address. The homes here represent the newest wave of Paradise Valley architecture — desert contemporary estates with flat rooflines, walls of glass, weathered steel and stone accents, and infinity-edge pools that frame mountain views. This is not the Mediterranean villa or Tuscan estate aesthetic of older PV communities; Azure is forward-looking and architecturally ambitious. The community's central location means the Phoenician resort and spa, Mountain Shadows, Sanctuary on Camelback Mountain, and the JW Marriott Scottsdale Camelback Inn are all within a 10-minute drive. The Borgata provides boutique shopping and dining at the base of Mummy Mountain. Camelback Mountain's Echo Canyon and Cholla Trail trailheads are accessible within minutes.

Azure's central Paradise Valley position delivers some of the shortest commute times of any PV community. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are just 7 minutes south via Scottsdale Road or Invergordon — among the fastest from any guard-gated PV address. Biltmore Fashion Park is 12 minutes west via Lincoln Drive. AJ's Fine Foods at Lincoln and Scottsdale is 3 minutes away. The Borgata is a 5-minute drive for dining and boutique shopping. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 20 minutes south via the 44th Street corridor or Scottsdale Road. Paradise Valley Country Club and Camelback Golf Club are both within 5 minutes. Mountain Shadows resort is a neighbor. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 25 minutes northeast via Shea Boulevard.

narrative.tagline: On the Fairway. Above the Rest.
narrative.summary: Exclusive gated community with stunning views of Camelback Country Club and surrounding mountains. Cullum Homes custom builds.

body.lead: Ironwood Golf Villas commands a prime position overlooking the Camelback Golf Club's fairways in northeastern Paradise Valley. With direct golf course frontage, guard-gated privacy, and a 7-minute drive to Fashion Square, this community offers the rare combination of a golf lifestyle and immediate access to Scottsdale's most dynamic shopping and dining district.

sections.content: Ironwood Golf Villas sits along the eastern edge of Paradise Valley, where the town's estate character meets the Scottsdale Road corridor. The community wraps around portions of the Camelback Golf Club's Indian Bend and Padre courses, giving many properties direct fairway views with irrigated greens creating a lush visual buffer. Mummy Mountain rises to the west, and Camelback Mountain's distinctive humped profile is visible to the southwest. The Scottsdale Road position — just minutes from Fashion Square — makes this one of the most commercially convenient addresses in Paradise Valley. Guard-gated entry ensures privacy while the internal streets are lined with mature trees and estate landscaping.

Ironwood Golf Villas bridges two distinct lifestyles: the golf-centric estate living of the Camelback corridor and the urban energy of the Scottsdale Road scene. Residents step out to fairway views and the Camelback Golf Club's 36 holes, yet are 7 minutes from Fashion Square's luxury retail, Old Town's galleries, and Scottsdale's restaurant scene. Homes range from elegantly updated villas to fully reimagined contemporary residences, many with resort-style pools, putting greens, and outdoor living rooms designed around the golf course panorama. Mountain Shadows resort, with its par-3 course and restaurant, is a close neighbor. The Phoenician resort and Sanctuary on Camelback Mountain are both within 10 minutes for spa and dining.

Ironwood Golf Villas' eastern PV position makes daily logistics remarkably efficient. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are just 7 minutes south on Scottsdale Road — one of the fastest commutes from any PV guard-gated community. Kierland Commons and Scottsdale Quarter are 12 minutes north for additional shopping and dining. AJ's Fine Foods at Lincoln and Scottsdale is under 5 minutes. Biltmore Fashion Park is 17 minutes west via Lincoln Drive through the scenic PV corridor. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 20 minutes south via the 101. Camelback Golf Club is at the doorstep. Mountain Shadows resort and its short course are a 3-minute drive. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 23 minutes northeast via Shea Boulevard.

narrative.summary: One of PV's most exclusive communities. Rare combination of newer construction and prime location near Scottsdale border.

body.lead: Judson Estates represents the next generation of Paradise Valley luxury — a ground-up community of custom estates designed for buyers who want new construction at the highest level without compromising on location. Positioned in central Paradise Valley with entry prices starting at $5 million, this guard-gated enclave delivers contemporary architecture and modern amenities on one-acre-plus lots just 8 minutes from Fashion Square.

sections.content: Judson Estates occupies a prime central Paradise Valley position along Cactus Wren Road, between Mummy Mountain to the north and Camelback Mountain to the south. The community's location places it within the town's most established estate corridor, surrounded by generational PV addresses — yet the homes themselves are entirely new. The terrain is classic Paradise Valley: gently rolling desert with native landscaping, mountain views in multiple directions, and the quiet, low-density character that defines the town. Guard-gated entry provides privacy, and the internal layout maximizes lot sizes while preserving natural desert buffers between properties. The Borgata and Lincoln Drive's restaurant row are minutes away.

Judson Estates is built for buyers who have outgrown renovation projects and want a home designed from the ground up to modern standards. Every estate is custom-built with the latest in smart-home technology, energy efficiency, and architectural design — desert contemporary with clean lines, dramatic ceiling heights, walls of glass, and seamless indoor-outdoor living. Properties typically include resort-caliber pools with spa features, outdoor kitchens, fire features, and guest casitas. The community appeals to tech executives, entrepreneurs, and luxury buyers relocating from coastal markets who expect homes to perform as well as they look. The Phoenician resort, Mountain Shadows, and Sanctuary on Camelback are all within 10 minutes for dining and spa.

Judson Estates' central PV location delivers excellent connectivity. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 8 minutes east via Scottsdale Road. Biltmore Fashion Park is 16 minutes west via Lincoln Drive. AJ's Fine Foods at Lincoln and Scottsdale is under 5 minutes. The Borgata is a 5-minute drive for boutique dining and shopping. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 20 minutes south. Paradise Valley Country Club and Camelback Golf Club are both within 5 minutes. Mountain Shadows resort is a close neighbor. The Phoenician's golf course and spa are under 10 minutes. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 22 minutes northeast via Shea Boulevard.

narrative.summary: 590 acres within the 7,100-acre Vistancia master plan. Currently 650+ homes at buildout.

body.lead: Blackstone at Vistancia is a guard-gated golf community within Peoria's Vistancia master plan, centered on an 18-hole Rees Jones-designed championship course. With custom estates from $1M to $3M+ on premium desert lots, Blackstone is the luxury anchor of the west Valley's largest master-planned development -- offering private club living, Sonoran Desert views, and proximity to Lake Pleasant, with the Vistancia Village retail center five minutes away.

sections.content: Blackstone occupies the northern reaches of the Vistancia master plan, where the terrain rises into the Sonoran Desert foothills above the developed valley floor. The Rees Jones course winds through natural desert washes, saguaro groves, and rocky outcroppings with views of the White Tank Mountains, the Bradshaw Range, and Lake Pleasant to the north. Custom homesites range from half-acre to one-acre-plus, with homes from $1M to $3M+ in desert contemporary and Southwestern styles. The guard-gated entry separates Blackstone from the broader Vistancia community. The community sits at approximately 1,600 feet elevation with expansive views in every direction.

Blackstone's private country club provides the social and recreational anchor -- championship golf, a clubhouse with dining and bar, fitness center, pool, and a full events calendar. The Vistancia Village retail center is five minutes south with groceries (Fry's, Sprouts), restaurants, and daily services. Lake Pleasant Regional Park is approximately twenty-seven minutes north via Loop 303 for boating, fishing, and waterfront recreation. Loop 303 is twenty-one minutes south, connecting to I-17 and the broader freeway network. Loop 101 is twenty-eight minutes southeast. Sky Harbor is forty-two minutes. The community's west Valley position means longer drives to Scottsdale, but for buyers focused on desert golf, space, and value, the trade-off is deliberate.

Mornings at Blackstone start on the course or the club's practice facility before the afternoon heat. The clubhouse handles breakfast and lunch. The Vistancia Village center -- Fry's, Sprouts, CVS, restaurants -- is five minutes for daily errands. The Peoria Regional Preserve trailheads are adjacent to Vistancia for hiking and mountain biking. Medical care comes from HonorHealth Sonoran Crossing, approximately fifteen minutes south, or Banner Thunderbird, about twenty-five minutes. Schools in the Peoria Unified School District -- one of the state's top-rated districts -- serve the community. Evening dining includes the clubhouse, the growing Vistancia restaurant scene, or the P83 Entertainment District in downtown Peoria, about twenty-five minutes south.

golf.description: | Blackstone Country Club — Jim Engh-designed 18-hole championship course | Troon Privé-managed (Troon's ultra-premium private club brand) | Golf Digest "Best in State" every year since opening | Travel + Leisure "Top 100 Golf Communities" | Desert target-style golf with dramatic elevation changes and panoramic mountain views | Full practice facilities, private instruction, pro shop | Club membership is purchased separately from home

narrative.summary: Exclusive gated sanctuary of only 14 lots within the WestWing Mountain master plan. Elevated desert-contemporary customs.

body.lead: Alvamar is an ultra-luxury hillside estate community on WestWing Mountain in North Peoria, where custom homes from $2M to $3.5M+ occupy ridgeline homesites with 360-degree views of the White Tank Mountains, the Sonoran Preserve, and the Valley below. With one-to-three-acre lots and no shared golf amenity to maintain, Alvamar is designed for buyers who want architectural freedom and mountaintop privacy.

sections.content: Alvamar occupies the upper slopes and ridgeline of WestWing Mountain in North Peoria. The topography is dramatic -- granite outcroppings, native desert vegetation, and elevation changes that position homes above the valley floor with unobstructed views in all directions. The White Tank Mountains anchor the western horizon, the Bradshaw Range extends to the north, and the city lights of the Valley spread south and east. Lots range from one to three acres, allowing custom architecture, pools, guest casitas, and the kind of outdoor living spaces that the setting demands. Homes are $2M to $3.5M+ in predominantly desert contemporary styles.

Alvamar has no golf course, no clubhouse, and no community amenity campus -- the lifestyle is the mountaintop itself. Views, privacy, space, and architectural quality define the community. For golf, the nearby Blackstone Country Club and Trilogy at Vistancia provide options within ten to fifteen minutes. The Vistancia and Lake Pleasant Parkway retail corridors offer groceries and dining ten minutes north. Loop 101 is fifteen minutes south for access to the broader metro. Loop 303 is eighteen minutes southwest. Sky Harbor is thirty-seven minutes. The Peoria Regional Preserve and the Sunrise Mountain trailheads are adjacent for hiking.

Daily life at Alvamar revolves around the home and the setting. Morning coffee with mountain views, a hike on the Sunrise Mountain trails, or a drive to Blackstone or Trilogy for golf. The Sprouts and Fry's on Lake Pleasant Parkway handle groceries, about ten minutes north. The growing restaurant scene along Happy Valley Road and in the Vistancia Village center provides dining options without a long drive. Medical care comes from HonorHealth Sonoran Crossing or Banner Thunderbird, both approximately twenty minutes. Schools in Peoria Unified serve the area. Evening entertainment is typically at home -- where the sunset views over the White Tanks and the city-light panorama are the main attraction.

hoa.details: $100–$250/mo. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

narrative.summary: Home to the two most expensive sales in Peoria history — both exceeding $5 million ($5.275M in May 2024). Ultra-luxury custom territory that rivals Scottsdale pricing.

body.lead: Casa De Sunrise is an exclusive hilltop estate enclave on Sunrise Mountain in Peoria, where a handful of custom homes from $3M to $5.3M+ occupy the mountain's upper slopes with commanding views of the Valley, the White Tank Mountains, and the Sonoran Desert. With lots from one to five acres and guard-gated privacy, Casa De Sunrise is the west Valley's most exclusive residential address.

sections.content: Casa De Sunrise occupies the upper elevations of Sunrise Mountain in central North Peoria, where the mountain's rocky slopes provide natural separation from the developed valley below. The homesites -- one to five acres -- are positioned on the mountain's ridgeline and south-facing slopes, commanding panoramic views that extend from the White Tank Mountains in the west to the Four Peaks and Superstition range in the east. Homes are custom-built architectural estates from $3M to $5.3M+, designed to integrate with the mountain terrain. The guard-gated entry ensures privacy. The community is small -- a limited number of homesites preserve the mountain's character.

Casa De Sunrise is about the setting and the exclusivity. There is no golf course or community clubhouse -- the mountaintop is the amenity. For golf, the Blackstone Country Club, Trilogy Golf Club, and Westwing Mountain Golf Club are within fifteen minutes. Loop 101 at Happy Valley Road is ten minutes south, providing access to the east Valley and the freeway network. The Arrowhead Towne Center -- the west Valley's premier shopping destination -- is twelve minutes south. Sky Harbor is thirty-two minutes. Loop 303 is fifteen minutes west. The community attracts buyers from the west Valley's business community and professionals who work along the I-17 corridor.

Morning routines at Casa De Sunrise start with the views -- sunrise over the Valley from a mountaintop terrace is the daily reward for living at elevation. A hike on the Sunrise Mountain trails, which are accessible from the community, or a drive to one of the nearby golf clubs fills the active hours. Groceries come from the AJ's, Fry's, or Sprouts along Happy Valley Road, about ten minutes south. The Arrowhead corridor provides more extensive shopping and dining. Medical care comes from HonorHealth or Banner Thunderbird, both within twenty minutes. Schools in the Peoria Unified district serve families. Evening entertaining is almost exclusively at home -- the mountaintop setting and the views make leaving optional.

pois.name: New River Trail Head At Westbrook Village

narrative.summary: Peoria's most expensive gated subdivision outside Vistancia. Last two closings at $3.6M and $3.8M.

body.lead: Copper Creek Estates is a guard-gated custom estate enclave in North Peoria, where a small collection of homes from $3M to $3.8M+ on one-to-two-acre lots sit in the desert foothills with views of the Sonoran Preserve, the Bradshaw Mountains, and the Valley below. With a resort-style community pool and direct access to Loop 101, Copper Creek combines ultra-luxury living with the west Valley's most convenient freeway position.

sections.content: Copper Creek Estates occupies a premium desert foothill position in North Peoria, between the Sonoran Mountain Ranch development and the open desert of the Sonoran Preserve. The community is small and guard-gated, with lots from one to two acres and homes from $3M to $3.8M+. The terrain provides natural elevation with views of the surrounding mountains and the Valley's city lights. Architecture is predominantly desert contemporary -- clean lines, native stone, and large glass walls that capture the views. The community's resort-style pool and gathering area serve as the shared amenity.

Copper Creek's position in eastern North Peoria provides the best freeway access of any ultra-luxury community in the west Valley. Loop 101 at Happy Valley Road is ten minutes south, connecting directly to the I-17 corridor and the broader metro. I-17 is twelve minutes east. Sky Harbor is thirty minutes -- competitive with many North Scottsdale communities. The Arrowhead Towne Center is fourteen minutes for shopping. The Sonoran Preserve trailheads are adjacent for hiking. The nearby golf options include Westwing Mountain Golf Club and the private clubs within Vistancia, all within fifteen minutes.

Daily life at Copper Creek blends desert estate living with practical urban access. Morning hikes into the Sonoran Preserve, a workout at a nearby fitness facility, or a round at one of the area's golf courses. Groceries come from the Fry's, Sprouts, or AJ's along Happy Valley Road, about ten minutes south. The growing restaurant scene on Happy Valley Road and in the Norterra corridor provides dining options. Medical care is available at HonorHealth Sonoran Crossing (twelve minutes) or Banner Thunderbird (twenty minutes). Peoria Unified schools serve the area. The community's small scale -- perhaps twenty to thirty homes total -- creates an intimate neighborhood where every resident is a neighbor, not a stranger.

identity.name: DC Ranch
identity.gating: Mix of gated and non-gated neighborhoods; Silverleaf is 24/7 guard-gated

narrative.tagline: The Ranch That Became a Kingdom
narrative.summary: 4,400-acre master-planned community spanning four distinctive villages. Originally a historic cattle ranch ("Desert Camp" brand from 1880s).

body.lead: DC Ranch is one of North Scottsdale’s largest and most diverse master-planned communities, spanning more than 4,600 acres at the base of the McDowell Mountains. With over 20 distinct neighborhoods—from paired villas starting around $600K to the guard-gated estates of Silverleaf above $20M—DC Ranch accommodates a wider range of buyers than almost any community in the corridor while maintaining a cohesive standard of design and landscaping throughout.

sections.content: DC Ranch stretches from the Pima Road corridor east into the foothills of the McDowell Mountains, where Thompson Peak and its ridgeline provide a dramatic backdrop visible from nearly every neighborhood. The master plan preserves natural desert washes, boulder outcroppings, and stands of mature saguaro as open space between residential clusters. Architectural styles range from desert contemporary to Tuscan-influenced, with guidelines that keep palette and scale consistent across the community. Silverleaf, the crown-jewel enclave within DC Ranch, occupies the highest elevations with sweeping city-light and mountain views, while neighborhoods like Country Club, Desert Parks, and Desert Camp Village offer more accessible entry points without compromising quality.

The Country Club at DC Ranch anchors the recreational experience. Scott Miller’s 18-hole championship course weaves through natural desert terrain, and the club’s mission of “Fun, Family, and Friendliness” sets a tone that’s competitive on the course but welcoming off it. Beyond golf, DC Ranch Village—the community’s commercial hub—puts AJ’s Fine Foods, restaurants, boutiques, and services within walking distance for many residents. The community also operates an extensive trail system, parks, splash pads, and community gathering spaces. Market Street at DC Ranch adds dining options like Kona Grill, Pita Jungle, and seasonal farmers’ markets. Silverleaf members access a separate, ultra-private club with its own dining, spa, and fitness facilities.

DC Ranch’s central North Scottsdale position delivers strong connectivity. Kierland Commons and Desert Ridge Marketplace are both about 10–15 minutes away for shopping and dining. Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea campus is a 20-minute drive, and Fashion Square and Old Town are roughly 25 minutes south via Pima Road or the 101. Loop 101 access is about 10–15 minutes from most neighborhoods, connecting to employment corridors across the valley. Schools include Scottsdale Unified options like Copper Ridge Elementary, Pinnacle Peak Elementary, and Desert Mountain High School, alongside private choices such as Notre Dame Preparatory and Scottsdale Christian Academy.

hoa.details: $200–$500/mo (Community Council + Ranch Association + neighborhood varies). Club at DC Ranch dues separate. 0.5% capital fee at closing. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

residential.neighborhoods: | Country Club Village — centered around the golf course, mix of homes | Silverleaf Village — ultra-luxury guard-gated enclave (detailed in separate entry above) | Desert Camp Village — near the Desert Camp community center | Desert Park Village — diverse housing types, family-oriented | All but one enclave is gated or guard-gated

golf.description: | The Country Club at DC Ranch — Tom Lehman & John Fought designed 18-hole, par-71 championship course | Slopes ranging from 123 to 137 — designed for all skill levels | Stunning views of desert, McDowell Mountains, and city lights from every hole | Cart or walking options throughout | Full practice facilities and pro shop | See also: Silverleaf Club (separate, guard-gated — Tom Weiskopf course, detailed above)

pois.name: The Country Club at DC Ranch

narrative.tagline: Ten Degrees Cooler. A World Apart.
narrative.summary: 713 acres at 3,000 ft elevation — often 10 degrees cooler than Phoenix. 339 homesites total.

body.lead: Mirabel is one of the most exclusive residential golf communities in the American Southwest. Positioned in Far North Scottsdale at roughly 2,700 feet of elevation, this 713-acre enclave limits membership to just 300 households—a cap that preserves both the pace of play on its Tom Fazio course and the intimate character of the community itself. Homes range from $1.5M to well beyond $10M.

sections.content: Mirabel occupies a stretch of pristine Upper Sonoran Desert where the terrain transitions from rolling desert floor to the rocky foothills below Continental Mountain and the Tonto National Forest boundary. The elevation delivers cooler temperatures than valley-floor communities, and sightlines reach from Four Peaks and the Mazatzal Range to the east all the way to Camelback Mountain and the Phoenix skyline to the south. Homesites range from one to five-plus acres, and architectural guidelines demand that residences integrate with the native landscape—low-profile rooflines, desert-tone masonry, and extensive use of glass that frames the views without fighting the terrain. Native washes, saguaro groves, and ironwood corridors flow through the community as permanent open space.

Mirabel Golf Club is the centerpiece. Tom Fazio’s par-72 layout stretches 7,189 yards through boulder-strewn desert, and the 300-member cap means tee times are rarely crowded and rounds are walked at a leisurely pace with forecaddies. The clubhouse—a 32,000-square-foot hacienda-style facility—houses fine and casual dining, a wine room, private event space, and a fully equipped fitness center. Outside, residents have access to tennis and pickleball courts, a resort-style pool complex, a spa, and a children’s activity area. The social calendar runs year-round: wine dinners, holiday galas, guest chef series, and member tournaments that reinforce the community’s reputation as a place where people know one another by name.

Mirabel’s Far North Scottsdale location trades urban proximity for privacy and space. Desert Ridge Marketplace, the closest major retail center, is about 25–30 minutes south, while Loop 101 sits roughly 30 minutes away. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are a 35–40 minute drive, as is Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea Boulevard campus. The nearby towns of Carefree and Cave Creek—just 10 minutes west—offer boutique shopping, galleries, and locally favored restaurants along Easy Street and Cave Creek Road. Scottsdale Airport handles private jet traffic about 25 minutes south. Schools include Scottsdale Unified’s Desert Mountain High School, Cactus Shadows in Cave Creek USD, and private options like Rancho Solano Preparatory.

golf.description: | Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole championship course (par 71, 7,147 yards) | Golfweek Top 100 private course (#76 nationally) | Generous fairways with strategic bunkering, famous long par-4s on holes 9 and 18 | Water feature on hole 10, spectacular Pinnacle Peak backdrop on hole 11 | Bent A4 grass greens ("best greens in the Valley" per members) | Active caddie program (among most prestigious clubs nationally) | Walkable course design — cart optional | Full practice facilities: driving range, chipping/putting greens, short game area | Membership strictly capped at 275 Golf + 50 Social to preserve pace and experience
golf.membership: Invitation/application process. Golf and Sports Social categories. Waitlist in effect due to membership cap.

pois.name: The Cantina at Legend Trail Golf Club

narrative.tagline: North Scottsdale's Most Active Lifestyle Address
narrative.summary: 823 acres, Del Webb-planned, 23 neighborhoods, 1,380 homes. Member-owned country club.

body.lead: Terravita sits at the foot of Black Mountain in the far northwest corner of Scottsdale, where 823 guard-gated acres deliver an active-adult lifestyle that balances serious recreation with the social warmth of a community where neighbors genuinely know each other. Homes range from $500K to $2.5M—a price band that attracts retirees, semi-retired professionals, and second-home buyers who want quality golf and fitness without the seven-figure initiation fees of neighboring clubs.

sections.content: Terravita wraps around the base of Black Mountain, a 3,398-foot volcanic landmark that anchors the northern edge of the community and provides a dramatic backdrop visible from virtually every street. The terrain slopes gently through natural desert washes lined with palo verde, ironwood, and native wildflowers that erupt in spring color. Residential architecture leans Mediterranean and desert contemporary, with earth-tone stucco, stone accents, and tile roofs that integrate into the hillside palette. Lot sizes vary from courtyard-style homes on smaller footprints to custom builds on half-acre parcels with direct mountain views. The adjacent Cave Creek Regional Park and Black Mountain Preserve add thousands of acres of undeveloped open space immediately outside the gates.

Terravita Golf Club is the community’s signature draw. The Billy Casper and Greg Nash design stretches 7,075 yards through dramatic elevation changes, and a recent Phil Smith renovation brought the greens to USGA specification. Unusually for a private club, members use personal golf carts—a perk that adds convenience and a sense of ownership to the daily round. Beyond golf, the community operates a 25,000-square-foot clubhouse with fitness center, heated pool and spa, tennis courts, bocce, and a robust social calendar that includes wine tastings, themed dinners, and holiday events. Hiking trails thread through the community and connect to the broader Cave Creek trail network, with Black Mountain Summit—a 1.2-mile climb with 360-degree views—starting right at the community’s edge.

Terravita’s northwest position places it between the bustle of Scottsdale proper and the small-town character of Cave Creek, which is less than 10 minutes west along Cave Creek Road for restaurants, galleries, and the Frontier Town entertainment district. Desert Ridge Marketplace is about 20–25 minutes south for major retail and dining chains. Loop 101 sits roughly 25–30 minutes away, and Fashion Square and Old Town are a 35–40 minute drive. Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea campus is about 35 minutes southeast. Day-to-day shopping is handled by the Shops at Dove Valley and the commercial nodes along Scottsdale Road and Cave Creek Road. Schools in the area are served by Cave Creek Unified, including Cactus Shadows High School, alongside private options like Rancho Solano and Notre Dame Preparatory.

hoa.details: $200–$400/mo. Includes community amenities; golf dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: Black Mtn Black Mountain Elementary School

identity.name: Troon North
identity.gating: Mix of gated enclaves and non-gated areas (13 neighborhoods)

narrative.summary: 1,800 acres with 13 unique neighborhoods spanning from production homes to ultra-luxury custom estates. World-renowned desert golf destination — often cited as where "desert golf" was perfected.

body.lead: Troon North is one of the defining residential communities in North Scottsdale—a 1,800-acre master plan that wraps around the base of Pinnacle Peak and encompasses 13 distinct neighborhoods ranging from accessible townhome enclaves to ultra-private custom estates. Two championship golf courses, dramatic desert terrain, and a price spectrum from $700K to over $8M make it one of the most diverse yet cohesive luxury addresses in the Valley.

sections.content: Pinnacle Peak—the 3,170-foot granite landmark that dominates North Scottsdale’s skyline—rises directly above the community, and its silhouette is visible from nearly every neighborhood within Troon North. The terrain is classic Upper Sonoran: boulder fields, saguaro forests, natural desert washes, and elevation changes that architects use to create homes with layered views of mountains, city lights, and open desert. The 13 neighborhoods range from intimate gated enclaves like Candlewood, Sincuidados, and The Boulders Enclave to the broader streetscapes of Troon Village and Pinnacle Peak Vistas. Architectural guidelines ensure consistency without uniformity—desert contemporary, territorial, and modern styles all appear, unified by materials and colors drawn from the landscape.

Troon North Golf Club is the community’s anchor and one of the most recognized desert courses in the world. Tom Weiskopf’s Monument Course—famous for its boulder-strewn fairways and the iconic third hole—has appeared on virtually every “best of” list since opening in 1990. The Pinnacle Course adds a second 18 with elevated tees and sweeping valley panoramas. Both courses are open to resort play, which gives the community a more dynamic atmosphere than fully private clubs. Beyond golf, Pinnacle Peak Park’s 1.75-mile summit trail starts at the community’s edge, and the broader McDowell Sonoran Preserve trail network is a short drive away. Dining options cluster along the Troon and Pinnacle Peak corridors: Talavera at Four Seasons, Deseo, and Toro Latin Kitchen are all nearby.

Troon North’s position along Dynamite Boulevard and Happy Valley Road puts Desert Ridge Marketplace about 20–25 minutes west for everyday shopping, dining, and entertainment. Loop 101 is roughly the same distance, connecting residents to Tempe, Chandler, and the airport corridor. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are a 30–35 minute drive south via Pima Road, as is Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea Boulevard campus. Scottsdale Airport handles private aviation about 20 minutes south. Daily needs—groceries, pharmacy, coffee—are met by the commercial centers along Pima and Dynamite, including AJ’s Fine Foods and the shops at Pinnacle Peak Village. Schools include Desert Mountain High School (Scottsdale Unified) and private options like Scottsdale Preparatory and Notre Dame Prep.

hoa.details: $150–$500/mo (varies by neighborhood — 13 distinct enclaves with different HOAs). Golf Club dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

Frequently ranked among top public/resort courses in the U.S. | Pinnacle Course — Tom Weiskopf design, more forgiving than Monument but equally scenic. pois.name: Four Seasons Resort Scottsdale at Troon North

narrative.summary: 1,400 acres with 12 unique subdivisions. Custom, semi-custom, patio and townhomes.

body.lead: Troon Village is a gated residential enclave nestled into the foothills south of Pinnacle Peak, anchored by the private Troon Golf & Country Club. Homes range from $800K to over $5M, with custom estates occupying the higher elevations and production-luxury homes filling the lower slopes. The community’s elevated position delivers panoramic views—Pinnacle Peak to the north, city lights to the south, and the McDowell Mountains to the east.

sections.content: Troon Village occupies the hillsides south of Pinnacle Peak where the terrain rises from the desert floor into rocky, saguaro-studded foothills. Streets follow natural grades rather than grid patterns, and homesites take advantage of elevation changes to create tiered views. Architectural styles lean desert contemporary and Southwest territorial, with natural stone, copper accents, and earth-tone stucco that blends into the surrounding landscape. Many properties feature wrap-around patios, infinity pools, and outdoor kitchens oriented toward the valley views. The community’s gated perimeter and low density preserve a sense of seclusion, even though the Troon corridor’s restaurants and services sit just minutes away along Dynamite Boulevard.

Troon Golf & Country Club is the community’s centerpiece—a private, member-only club with an 18-hole championship course that threads through natural desert terrain. The clubhouse offers fine and casual dining, a lounge, fitness center, and a pool complex. The social calendar runs year-round with member tournaments, wine dinners, holiday parties, and themed events that keep the community connected. Beyond the gates, Pinnacle Peak Park’s summit trail starts nearby, and the broader Troon corridor offers dining at spots like El Encanto, Greasewood Flat, and the restaurants clustered around Troon North. For those who want a second course, Troon North Golf Club’s two public-access championship layouts are just up the road.

Troon Village’s position in the Troon corridor means everyday errands require a short drive to the commercial nodes along Pima Road and Dynamite Boulevard—AJ’s Fine Foods, pharmacies, and coffee shops are all within 10 minutes. Desert Ridge Marketplace is about 20–25 minutes west for larger retail and dining. Loop 101 sits roughly the same distance, connecting to Tempe, Chandler, and the airport corridor. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are a 30–35 minute drive south, as is Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea Boulevard campus. Scottsdale Airport is about 20 minutes for private aviation. Schools are served by Scottsdale Unified, with Desert Mountain High School as the designated secondary school, plus private options like Scottsdale Preparatory and Notre Dame Prep.

identity.name: Gainey Ranch
identity.gating: 24/7 guarded entry (north side communities are guard-gated or electronic-gated; south side communities have electronic gates)

narrative.tagline: From Arabian Horses to Arizona Aristocracy
narrative.summary: Former Arabian horse ranch of Daniel C. Gainey.

body.lead: Gainey Ranch is one of Scottsdale's most recognized luxury addresses — a 640-acre community of lakes, golf fairways, and estate homes anchored by the private Gainey Ranch Golf Club. Established in 1985 and stretching along Doubletree Ranch Road, Gainey Ranch offers a mature, resort-caliber setting with homes from $500K to $5M+, just five minutes from Kierland Commons and thirteen from Fashion Square.

sections.content: Gainey Ranch occupies a rectangular parcel between Scottsdale Road and Hayden Road, centered on Doubletree Ranch Road. The community's three nine-hole golf courses — Arroyo, Lakes, and Dunes — weave through the property, creating a landscape of manicured fairways, nine interconnected lakes, and palm-lined pathways. Homes span a wide range: from $500K patio homes and condominiums in The Estates to $5M+ waterfront and fairway custom residences. The architecture is predominantly Southwest contemporary, with many homes featuring courtyards, water features, and indoor-outdoor living designed around the golf and lake views.

Gainey Ranch's private golf club is the community's social centerpiece — 27 holes of championship golf, a resort-style pool and fitness center, and a full-service clubhouse with dining. The nine lakes support kayaking, paddleboarding, and catch-and-release fishing. Kierland Commons — Scottsdale's premier mixed-use destination with dining, retail, and the Westin Kierland Resort — is five minutes north. Fashion Square is thirteen minutes south. Sky Harbor is twenty-two minutes via the 101 and 202. Loop 101 access is just three minutes at the Gainey Ranch Road interchange, making cross-Valley commutes straightforward.

Daily rhythms at Gainey Ranch follow the golf and social calendar. Morning tee times, a swim at the club pool, or a walk along the greenbelt pathways that connect the community's lakes and parks. Groceries come from the Whole Foods or AJ's Fine Foods on Scottsdale Road, both within five minutes. Kierland Commons handles shopping and dining — North Italia, Zinburger, and Postino are resident favorites. Schools in the Scottsdale Unified District serve the community, with Chaparral High and private options like Notre Dame Preparatory nearby. HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea is five minutes on Shea Boulevard. The community's HOA maintains the lakes, common areas, and extensive landscaping to resort-level standards.

hoa.details: $200–$800/mo (varies significantly: condos/townhomes higher, estates lower). Estate Club membership included for all residents. Golf Club dues separate (Invited network). *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: Canton Dragon Asian Grill and Bar

identity.name: Grayhawk
identity.gating: Mix of guard-gated enclaves and non-gated areas

narrative.tagline: Championship Golf. Scottsdale's Best Social Scene.
narrative.summary: Two main neighborhoods: The Park and The Retreat Village. Condos to sprawling estates (1,800–6,000+ sq ft).

body.lead: Grayhawk is one of North Scottsdale’s most established master-planned communities, blending championship golf, family-friendly parks, and a diverse neighborhood mix across a broad swath of land straddling the Loop 101. Homes range from $500K condos and townhomes to $4M+ custom estates in guard-gated enclaves, making Grayhawk accessible to a wider range of buyers than most Scottsdale golf communities.

sections.content: Grayhawk spreads across the heart of North Scottsdale, bounded roughly by Thompson Peak Parkway to the east, Scottsdale Road to the west, and the Loop 101 to the south. The master plan integrates natural desert corridors, community parks, and trail connections between neighborhoods. Architectural styles vary by enclave—desert contemporary in the higher-end custom sections, Mediterranean and Santa Fe influences in the production-luxury neighborhoods, and modern urban designs in the newer attached-home communities. The McDowell Mountains provide a constant eastern backdrop, and the community’s relatively flat terrain makes it one of the most walkable and bikeable in the North Scottsdale corridor.

Grayhawk Golf Club anchors the recreational experience with two championship courses. The Raptor Course—originally a Tom Fazio design, later refreshed—is the signature layout, known for dramatic desert holes and tournament-quality conditioning. The Talon Course offers a complementary experience with more elevation change and risk-reward holes. Both courses are open to public play, which gives Grayhawk a more dynamic, resort-like atmosphere. Phil’s Grill at the clubhouse is a popular post-round gathering spot. Beyond golf, Grayhawk Community Park features playgrounds, sports courts, ramadas, and open fields. The community’s trail system connects to the broader Scottsdale path network. Nearby dining includes the restaurants at Scottsdale Quarter, Whole Foods Market, and the evolving culinary scene along Hayden Road.

Grayhawk’s central location is its biggest daily advantage. Desert Ridge Marketplace and Loop 101 are both about 10–15 minutes away, providing quick access to major retail, entertainment, and valley-wide commuting. Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea campus is roughly a 20-minute drive. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale sit 20–25 minutes south via Pima Road or the 101. Scottsdale Quarter, just across the 101, adds Restoration Hardware, Anthropologie, and a growing restaurant row within a 5-minute drive. Schools include Scottsdale Unified’s highly rated Grayhawk Elementary, Pinnacle Peak Elementary, and Desert Mountain High School. Private options include Basis Scottsdale, Notre Dame Prep, and Scottsdale Christian Academy.

hoa.details: $150–$400/mo (varies by neighborhood). Separate from golf/amenity fees. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: Eddie Merlot's Prime Aged Beef & Seafood

identity.name: Ancala
identity.city: East Scottsdale (south side of McDowell Mountains)

narrative.summary: Hillside lots with wonderful valley views. Custom homes 3,000–7,000 sq ft in diverse architectural styles.

body.lead: Ancala is a private country club community set against the south face of the McDowell Mountains in east Scottsdale. Anchored by the members-only Ancala Country Club, the community offers homes from $800K to $5M+ on lots that climb into the foothills, with sweeping views of the Valley below. Its position on the east side provides a quieter, more desert-immersive setting than the Scottsdale Road corridor communities.

sections.content: Ancala occupies the sloping terrain at the base of the McDowell Mountains, south of Shea Boulevard between 108th Street and the mountain preserve. The community rises from the valley floor into the foothills, giving higher-elevation lots dramatic panoramic views from Four Peaks to Camelback. The 18-hole championship course winds through natural desert washes and boulder formations, with the mountains as a constant backdrop. Homes range from $800K fairway-view patio homes to $5M+ hillside custom estates on acre-plus lots. The architecture trends toward desert contemporary and Southwest Territorial, with stone, copper, and earth-tone palettes that blend into the landscape.

Ancala is built around private club life. The Ancala Country Club offers championship golf, tennis courts, a fitness center, pool, and a full-service clubhouse with dining and social events. The private membership model keeps the course uncrowded and the community's social fabric tight. Fashion Square is twenty-two minutes west via Shea and the 101. Kierland Commons is twelve minutes. Sky Harbor is twenty-eight minutes via the 101 to 202. The McDowell Sonoran Preserve — one of the largest urban wilderness areas in the country — borders the community to the north, providing hundreds of miles of hiking and mountain biking trails.

Mornings at Ancala might begin with an early tee time on the uncrowded private course, a hike on the Gateway Trailhead in the nearby Sonoran Preserve, or a workout at the club's fitness center. Groceries come from the Bashas' or Sprouts on Shea, about eight minutes west, or the AJ's Fine Foods in Scottsdale Ranch. Schools in Scottsdale Unified serve the area, with Anasazi Elementary and Desert Mountain High among the options. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is approximately eight minutes via Shea and the 101. Evening dining gravitates toward the club's dining room for casual nights and the Kierland corridor for variety. The trade-off for Ancala is distance from Old Town — but residents choose this community precisely because they prefer mountain quiet over urban proximity.

narrative.tagline: 640 Acres of High Desert and Championship Golf
narrative.summary: 640 acres of high desert. Over half the land preserved as natural open space.

body.lead: Legend Trail occupies a quiet stretch of Far North Scottsdale where the desert opens up and the pace slows down. Anchored by a Rees Jones–designed golf course and surrounded by natural desert preserve, this community offers homes from $500K to over $2M in a mix of gated and non-gated neighborhoods. The atmosphere is relaxed and neighborly—closer in spirit to Carefree than to the busier enclaves along the 101 corridor.

sections.content: Legend Trail sits at approximately 2,500 feet of elevation in the transition zone between Scottsdale’s suburban edge and the open desert that stretches north toward Carefree and Cave Creek. The terrain is gently rolling, punctuated by natural washes, boulder outcroppings, and stands of mature saguaro that give the community its distinctive character. Homesites range from courtyard-style patio homes to custom lots of an acre or more, with architectural styles running from desert contemporary to Santa Fe territorial. Pinnacle Peak and the Continental Mountains provide the northern skyline, while Troon Mountain and the McDowell Range anchor views to the east and south. Desert corridors thread between neighborhoods, preserving wildlife habitat and sightlines.

Legend Trail Golf Club is a Rees Jones design that plays through natural desert terrain with generous fairways and well-defended greens. The club operates on a semi-private model—members enjoy priority access and social programming, while outside play keeps the course financially healthy without overcrowding. Recent renovations have updated the clubhouse, practice facilities, and course conditioning. Beyond golf, the community offers a heated pool, spa, fitness center, tennis and pickleball courts, and a social calendar that includes themed dinners, holiday celebrations, and member-guest tournaments. Hiking and mountain biking trails connect to the surrounding desert, and the nearby towns of Carefree and Cave Creek—about 10 minutes northwest—add galleries, restaurants, and a Friday-night farmers’ market.

Legend Trail’s Far North Scottsdale address means trading urban proximity for space and quiet. Desert Ridge Marketplace is about 20–25 minutes south for major retail and dining. Loop 101 sits roughly 25 minutes away, connecting to employment centers in Tempe, Chandler, and Phoenix. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are a 30–35 minute drive, as is Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea Boulevard campus. Day-to-day shopping—groceries, pharmacy, coffee—is handled by the commercial centers along Pima Road and Dynamite Boulevard, including AJ’s Fine Foods and Safeway. Schools are served by both Scottsdale Unified (Desert Mountain High School) and Cave Creek Unified (Cactus Shadows High School), with private options like Rancho Solano Preparatory and Notre Dame Prep nearby.

hoa.details: $150–$350/mo. Golf Club dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: The Cantina at Legend Trail Golf Club

narrative.tagline: Twelve Million Years in the Making
narrative.summary: Iconic boulder rock formations throughout — nowhere else in the Valley looks like this. Over half the land designated as natural open space.

body.lead: The Boulders is one of Arizona's most iconic luxury communities — a collection of homes woven among twelve-million-year-old granite boulder formations surrounding the Waldorf Astoria Boulders Resort and two championship Jay Morrish golf courses. With homes from $600K to $5M+ and a five-minute drive to Carefree's charming town center, The Boulders delivers a desert experience unlike anything else in the Valley.

sections.content: The Boulders occupies a landscape that feels primordial — house-sized granite boulders balanced in improbable formations, with homes and fairways carefully integrated around and between them. The two Jay Morrish-designed courses wind through desert washes and boulder fields that predate human habitation by millions of years. Homes range from $600K casitas and villas to $5M+ custom estates on multi-acre lots, many literally built into the rock formations. The Waldorf Astoria resort anchors the community with its 33,000-square-foot spa, multiple restaurants, pools, and the iconic boulder-framed main lodge. The setting is high desert at approximately 2,500 feet, with cooler temperatures than the valley floor.

Living at The Boulders means immersion in one of the desert's most extraordinary natural settings. The two golf courses offer challenging desert target play that ranks among Arizona's finest. The Waldorf Astoria spa, pools, and restaurants function as extended amenities for residents. Carefree's town center — with its sundial garden, galleries, and restaurants — is five minutes north. Cave Creek's Western-heritage dining scene is eight minutes northwest. Fashion Square is thirty-four minutes south via the 101. Sky Harbor is thirty-eight minutes. The CIVANA Wellness Resort in Carefree is ten minutes for an alternative spa experience. Spur Cross Ranch Conservation Area is fifteen minutes for hiking.

Mornings at The Boulders begin with the boulders themselves — the way early light paints the granite formations orange and gold is a daily ritual no photograph fully captures. A hike through the community's trail system or a tee time on the North Course follows. Coffee and breakfast at the resort's Palo Verde restaurant or a quick drive to Carefree's Black Mountain Coffee sets the day. Groceries come from the Bashas' in Carefree or the Sprouts at Carefree Crossroads, both within ten minutes. Medical care is available at HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea (twenty-five minutes) or the urgent care options in Cave Creek. Evening dining rotates between the resort's multiple venues, Carefree's Keeler's Neighborhood Steakhouse, and Cave Creek's legendary saloons.

hoa.details: $200–$600/mo (varies by sub-community and property type). Resort amenity access varies. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

identity.name: McCormick Ranch
identity.gating: Mix of gated and non-gated neighborhoods

narrative.summary: Scottsdale's first master-planned community. 4,300 acres, ~25,000 residents.

body.lead: McCormick Ranch is the community that invented the Scottsdale lifestyle — Arizona's first master-planned community, established in 1972 on the former McCormick family ranch. Two championship golf courses, seven lakes, and miles of greenbelt paths create a mature, livable community with homes from $400K to $4M+, ten minutes from Fashion Square and twenty from Sky Harbor.

sections.content: McCormick Ranch spans over 3,200 acres of central Scottsdale between Hayden Road and the Loop 101, anchored by the Pine and Palm championship courses. The landscape is uniquely mature for Scottsdale — fifty-plus years of growth have created a genuine tree canopy of mature pines, palms, and citrus that shades streets and pathways throughout the community. Seven interconnected lakes wind through the property, supporting waterfowl, fishing, and paddling. Homes range from $400K condominiums and patio homes to $4M+ lakefront and fairway estates. The community includes twenty parks, a community center, and an extensive path system.

McCormick Ranch's central Scottsdale position is its greatest asset. Fashion Square is ten minutes south via Scottsdale Road. Kierland Commons is eight minutes north. Sky Harbor is twenty minutes via the 101 to 202. Loop 101 access at Indian Bend or Shea provides fast east-west mobility. The two golf courses offer public daily-fee play — no membership required — making golf an accessible daily activity. The Indian Bend Wash greenbelt, running through the community, connects to the citywide path system for cycling and jogging. Chaparral Park, one of Scottsdale's largest, sits within the community.

McCormick Ranch life has a rhythm that decades of community building have refined. Morning jogs or bike rides along the Indian Bend Wash greenbelt connect through the entire community. The Pine Course clubhouse serves as an informal gathering spot. Groceries come from the numerous options along Scottsdale Road — Sprouts, Fry's, AJ's — all within five minutes. Schools in Scottsdale Unified are well-regarded, with Cochise Elementary, Mohave Middle, and Chaparral High serving the community. HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea is five minutes north. Evening dining ranges from the neighborhood spots along Hayden Road to Kierland's chef-driven restaurants. The community's maturity means no construction noise, no unsettled landscaping — just a working neighborhood that happens to have lakes and fairways.

hoa.details: $100–$400/mo (varies: condos higher, SFR lower). Includes lake access. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: Canton Dragon Asian Grill and Bar

narrative.summary: Scottsdale Country Club East 9 features guard-gated sections with golf course, lake, and mountain views.

body.lead: Scottsdale Country Club anchors one of central Scottsdale's most established neighborhoods, offering 18 holes of championship golf surrounded by mature estates in the McCormick Ranch corridor. At 14 minutes from both Fashion Square and Mayo Clinic, this private club community delivers the classic Scottsdale golf lifestyle with connectivity that north Scottsdale communities cannot match.

sections.content: Scottsdale Country Club occupies a generous footprint in central Scottsdale near Shea Boulevard and 77th Street, placing it squarely in the McCormick Ranch corridor — one of Scottsdale's most established and desirable residential areas. The community's championship golf course provides a green, irrigated landscape that defines the neighborhood character: tree-lined fairways, manicured greens, and the visual openness that only golf course frontage can deliver. Camelback Mountain is visible to the southwest, the McDowell Mountains to the northeast, and the Scottsdale skyline stretches in both directions along Scottsdale Road. Homes range from updated ranch estates to contemporary new builds on the course's most premium lots.

Scottsdale Country Club is a private member club, and membership is the social engine of the community. The club offers championship golf, tennis, fitness, swimming, and a robust dining and social calendar that creates genuine community among residents. Unlike the mega-communities of North Scottsdale where anonymity is easy, SCC is small enough that members know each other by name. Homes span a wide price range — from renovated $500K villas to $3M+ custom estates on premium fairway lots — making the community accessible to a broader range of luxury buyers than the ultra-exclusive enclaves further north. The location near McCormick Ranch provides additional amenities: walking paths along the Greenbelt, neighborhood parks, and a family-friendly atmosphere.

Scottsdale Country Club's central location is its defining practical advantage. Kierland Commons and Scottsdale Quarter are just 9 minutes north with Nordstrom, Apple, and dozens of restaurants. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 14 minutes south. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is only 14 minutes northeast — one of the shortest Mayo commutes from any golf community. The Loop 101 freeway is accessible within 5 minutes via Shea Boulevard, connecting to the entire metro. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 19 minutes south via the 101. AJ's Fine Foods, Sprouts, and Whole Foods are all within 7 minutes. Talking Stick Resort and Casino is a 10-minute neighbor. McCormick-Stillman Railroad Park is minutes away for families.

hoa.details: $150–$400/mo (varies: condos higher, SFR lower). Golf club dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

pois.name: Canton Dragon Asian Grill and Bar

narrative.summary: Exclusive community at the base of iconic Pinnacle Peak.

body.lead: Pinnacle Peak Country Club is a gated golf community positioned in central North Scottsdale, where Pima Road meets the foothills below Pinnacle Peak. Homes range from $800K to over $4M, and the community’s private 18-hole course and full-service clubhouse anchor a lifestyle that balances competitive golf with social warmth. The location—closer to the 101 than most Troon-area communities—gives residents shorter commutes without sacrificing the desert-mountain setting.

sections.content: Pinnacle Peak Country Club sits at the base of Pinnacle Peak’s southern slopes, where the terrain transitions from flat desert floor to the rocky foothills that define North Scottsdale’s skyline. The community’s homes ring the golf course, and many lots enjoy direct fairway frontage with views north toward the peak itself. Architectural styles are predominantly desert contemporary and Southwest territorial, with stone, stucco, and timber accents that harmonize with the surrounding landscape. Lot sizes range from quarter-acre patio homes to one-acre-plus custom estates. Natural desert washes and saguaro preserves flow between neighborhoods, maintaining the community’s connection to the landscape even in the more densely built sections.

The private 18-hole course is the community’s centerpiece—a desert layout that rewards shot-making and offers views of Pinnacle Peak, the McDowells, and the city lights from elevated tees. The clubhouse serves as the social hub, with fine and casual dining, a bar and lounge, card rooms, and event space for member functions. The fitness center, heated pool, and spa round out the wellness offerings, while tennis and pickleball courts see daily play. The social calendar runs year-round: couples’ nights, wine tastings, holiday galas, and member-guest tournaments that draw from the broader North Scottsdale golf community. Nearby, the Troon corridor’s restaurants and the Pinnacle Peak Patio’s legendary steakhouse are short drives away.

Pinnacle Peak CC’s central North Scottsdale position delivers solid connectivity. Desert Ridge Marketplace and Loop 101 are both about 10–15 minutes away, keeping commutes and errands manageable. Mayo Clinic Scottsdale’s Shea campus is a 20–25 minute drive, and Fashion Square and Old Town are roughly the same. Scottsdale Quarter—with Restoration Hardware, seasonal farmers’ markets, and upscale dining—sits just off the 101, about 10 minutes south. Daily essentials are covered by the commercial centers along Pima Road, including Whole Foods, Safeway, and AJ’s Fine Foods. Schools include Scottsdale Unified’s Desert Mountain High School, Basis Scottsdale, and Notre Dame Preparatory.

pois.name: Eddie Merlot's Prime Aged Beef & Seafood

narrative.summary: Part of the larger Anthem master-planned community. Robert Trent Jones II-designed public course also nearby.

body.lead: Anthem Country Club is the private, gated golf community within the Anthem master plan — two championship courses, a 70,000-square-foot clubhouse, and homes from $400K to $2M+ set in the high desert along the I-17 corridor. With direct freeway access five minutes away, the club delivers a private resort lifestyle while maintaining a workable commute to the Valley's employment centers.

sections.content: Anthem Country Club occupies the elevated western portion of the Anthem master plan, where the terrain rises toward Daisy Mountain and the surrounding desert foothills. The two Greg Nash-designed courses — Persimmon and Ironwood — wind through natural desert washes with saguaro-lined fairways and panoramic mountain views. Homes range from $400K fairway villas to $2M+ custom estates on larger lots. The guard-gated entry separates the club community from the broader Anthem development. The 70,000-square-foot clubhouse anchors social life with dining, fitness, tennis, a resort-style pool, and full-service spa.

Anthem Country Club offers a fully self-contained private club lifestyle. The two courses provide 36 holes of uncrowded play for members. The clubhouse dining room, grill, and bar serve as the primary social venue. Tennis courts, a fitness center, and the resort pool round out daily amenities. I-17 is five minutes east, providing a thirty-eight-minute freeway run to Sky Harbor and access to the Valley's north-south corridor. The Norterra shopping center — with Target, restaurants, and medical offices — is fifteen minutes south on I-17. Desert Ridge Marketplace is twenty-nine minutes for more extensive shopping.

Morning routines at Anthem Country Club follow the club calendar — early tee times, a workout in the fitness center, or laps in the heated pool. The club's dining options handle breakfast and lunch without leaving the gates. Groceries come from the Fry's or Safeway in the Anthem community center, about five minutes south, or the larger selection at Norterra. Schools in the Deer Valley Unified District serve the area, with Anthem School, Diamond Canyon, and Boulder Creek High among the options. Banner Health has a presence in Anthem with urgent care, and Banner Thunderbird Medical Center is accessible via I-17 in twenty minutes. Evening dining gravitates toward the clubhouse or the growing restaurant collection along the I-17 corridor at Norterra and Happy Valley Road.

hoa.details: $200–$400/mo. Club dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

narrative.summary: Prestigious Fountain Hills location. Stunning desert and mountain views.

body.lead: Eagle Mountain is a golf community in the southern foothills of Fountain Hills, centered on an 18-hole Scott Miller-designed course that climbs through the Sonoran Desert with panoramic views of the McDowell Mountains and Red Mountain. With homes from $500K to $3M+ and Fountain Hills' town center ten minutes north, Eagle Mountain delivers desert golf living with a practical connection to Scottsdale's Loop 101 corridor fifteen minutes west.

sections.content: Eagle Mountain occupies the rolling terrain of south Fountain Hills where the desert transitions from the McDowell Mountain foothills to the broader Sonoran landscape. The golf course is the community's defining feature — an 18-hole Scott Miller design that uses the natural desert washes and elevation changes to create a visually dramatic round with saguaro-lined fairways and mountain backdrops. Homes range from $500K attached villas to $3M+ custom estates, with many lots backing to the course or positioned on elevated pads with long-range views. The community sits at approximately 1,800 feet elevation, providing slightly cooler temperatures than the valley floor.

Eagle Mountain's lifestyle balances desert golf with the small-town character of Fountain Hills. The golf club operates as a semi-private facility, accessible to both residents and outside play. Fountain Hills' town center is ten minutes north — the Avenue of the Fountains, the famous fountain in Fountain Park, and a collection of galleries, restaurants, and shops. Loop 101 at Shea Boulevard is fifteen minutes west, opening up access to the broader Scottsdale corridor. Fashion Square and Old Town are twenty-six minutes. Sky Harbor is twenty-eight minutes via AZ-87 and the Loop 202. The McDowell Mountain Regional Park trailheads are accessible from the east, offering some of the Valley's best hiking and mountain biking.

Morning routines at Eagle Mountain often start on the course — early tee times before the afternoon heat, or a sunrise hike on the nearby McDowell trails. The Bashas' and Safeway on Shea Boulevard in Fountain Hills handle daily groceries, about eight minutes north. The town's restaurant scene — Phil's Filling Station, Havana Cafe, and the seasonal farmers market — provides local dining without the drive to Scottsdale. Medical care comes from HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, about twenty minutes west on Shea Boulevard. Schools in the Fountain Hills Unified School District serve the community with consistently strong ratings. For bigger shopping or dining excursions, Fashion Square and the Old Town Scottsdale restaurant scene are a manageable twenty-six-minute drive.

pois.name: The Grille at Eagle Mountain Golf Club

narrative.summary: Custom homes with luxury finishes. Mix of estate homes and low-maintenance patio homes.

body.lead: FireRock Country Club is a private, guard-gated golf community in the heart of Fountain Hills, built around an 18-hole Gary Panks-designed course that winds through dramatic red-rock formations and Sonoran Desert terrain. With homes from $800K to $5M+ and the full amenity package of a members-only club — clubhouse dining, fitness, pool, and spa — FireRock is Fountain Hills' premier private golf address.

sections.content: FireRock Country Club is set among the distinctive red-rock outcroppings that give the community its name — ancient granite formations that create a visual backdrop unlike any other golf community in the Valley. The Gary Panks-designed course is carved into this terrain, with holes playing through natural rock corridors, across desert washes, and along ridgelines with views of the McDowell Mountains, Four Peaks, and the Mazatzal Range. Homes range from $800K custom homes to $5M+ estates on elevated lots with panoramic views. The architecture blends desert contemporary and Southwestern styles, with most homes using native stone and earth tones that integrate with the natural surroundings.

FireRock operates as a fully private members-only club. The clubhouse serves as the social hub with dining, a bar and grill, fitness center, resort-style pool, spa, and tennis courts. The course offers uncrowded play for members — a key distinction from the semi-private facilities in the area. Fountain Hills' town center is nine minutes north, providing the galleries, restaurants, and community events centered on Fountain Park and the Avenue of the Fountains. Loop 101 is fifteen minutes west at Shea Boulevard, connecting to the broader Scottsdale corridor. Fashion Square is twenty-six minutes. Sky Harbor is twenty-eight minutes. The McDowell Mountain Regional Park is accessible from the north for hiking and trail riding.

Daily life at FireRock centers on the club. Morning golf, a workout in the fitness center, lunch at the clubhouse grill, or an afternoon by the pool. The guard-gated entry provides security and privacy. Fountain Hills' Bashas' and Safeway are about ten minutes for groceries. The town's dining options — from casual to upscale — are within nine minutes. Medical care comes from HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, about twenty minutes west. Fountain Hills Unified School District serves families in the area. The community's social calendar revolves around club events — member tournaments, holiday celebrations, wine dinners, and seasonal parties. For Scottsdale shopping and nightlife, Fashion Square and Old Town are a twenty-six-minute drive.

pois.name: The Grille at Eagle Mountain Golf Club

identity.name: SunRidge Canyon
identity.gating: Two gated enclaves (Solitude and Shadow Canyon); most neighborhoods non-gated

narrative.summary: 789 homes across 15 neighborhoods developed since 1996. McDowell Mountains serve as natural barrier protecting from over-development.

body.lead: SunRidge Canyon is a golf community in the northern foothills of Fountain Hills, built around an acclaimed 18-hole Keith Foster-designed course that plays through a natural desert canyon with dramatic elevation changes and mountain views. With homes from $600K to $3M+ and Fountain Hills' town center just six minutes south, SunRidge Canyon offers one of the Valley's most visually striking golf experiences paired with the small-town accessibility of Fountain Hills.

sections.content: SunRidge Canyon takes its name from the deep desert canyon that defines the golf course — a Keith Foster design that descends over 400 feet through natural rock formations, saguaro forests, and desert washes. The course has earned four-star ratings from Golf Digest and is consistently ranked among Arizona's top public courses. Homes range from $600K to $3M+, positioned on canyon rims, ridgelines, and elevated pads with views of the McDowell Mountains, the Mazatzal Range, and the Verde River Valley beyond. The community sits at approximately 2,000 feet elevation, with cooler temperatures than the valley floor and some of the most expansive views in the Fountain Hills area.

SunRidge Canyon's golf course operates as a daily-fee public facility, making it accessible to residents and visitors alike. The clubhouse offers dining and a pro shop. The community's position in upper Fountain Hills provides a sense of seclusion while remaining close to town — the Avenue of the Fountains, Fountain Park, galleries, and restaurants are just six minutes south. Loop 101 at Shea Boulevard is seventeen minutes west for Scottsdale access. Sky Harbor is twenty-nine minutes. The McDowell Mountain Regional Park and the Dixie Mine Trail are nearby for hiking and mountain biking. The community appeals to buyers who want serious desert golf paired with mountain privacy but not the isolation of more remote locations.

Mornings at SunRidge Canyon start with the views — sunrise over Four Peaks from a back patio, or an early tee time on the canyon course before the day heats up. Fountain Hills' town center is six minutes for groceries at Bashas' or Safeway, morning coffee, or a gallery walk along the Avenue of the Fountains. The community's elevation makes evening walks comfortable even in the warmer months. Medical care comes from HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, about twenty-two minutes west via Shea Boulevard. Fountain Hills Unified schools serve the area. Dining options in town range from casual to refined — Grapeables, Four Peaks Brewing, and the rotating food trucks in Fountain Park. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are thirty minutes for more extensive shopping and dining.

pois.name: The Grille at Eagle Mountain Golf Club

narrative.tagline: The Resort With Your Name on It
narrative.summary: Luxury villa community next door to CopperWynd Resort. Spacious 1,900–2,700 sq ft floor plans.

body.lead: The Villas at CopperWynd is a boutique luxury community perched on a ridgeline in Fountain Hills, adjacent to the CopperWynd Resort & Club. With homes from $700K to $1.5M and access to the resort's pool, spa, fitness, and dining amenities, CopperWynd delivers a resort-integrated lifestyle with panoramic views of the McDowell Mountains and the desert valley below.

sections.content: The Villas at CopperWynd occupy an elevated ridgeline position in the Fountain Hills foothills, adjacent to the CopperWynd Resort & Club. The topography is dramatic — homes sit at the edge of a natural ridgeline with unobstructed views in multiple directions: the McDowell Mountains to the west, Four Peaks and the Mazatzals to the northeast, and the city lights of the Valley below. Homes range from $700K to $1.5M in a variety of villa and custom formats, many with infinity-edge pools that appear to drop off into the desert below. The community is small and exclusive, with a limited number of residences sharing access to the resort's facilities.

CopperWynd's lifestyle is defined by its resort adjacency. Residents have access to the CopperWynd Resort & Club's amenities — a spa, heated pool, fitness center, tennis courts, and the Flourish restaurant with its panoramic terrace dining. The resort setting provides a hospitality layer that most residential communities cannot match. Fountain Hills' town center is eight minutes south for local shopping, galleries, and dining. Loop 101 at Shea Boulevard is sixteen minutes west, opening up Scottsdale's retail and dining corridor. Fashion Square is twenty-seven minutes. Sky Harbor is twenty-nine minutes. The community's hillside trails connect to the broader Fountain Hills trail network for hiking.

Daily life at CopperWynd blends resort luxury with desert quiet. Morning yoga at the resort spa, a swim in the heated pool, or coffee on a patio with mountain views in every direction. The resort's Flourish restaurant handles meals from breakfast through dinner without leaving the property. Groceries come from the Bashas' or Safeway in Fountain Hills, about eight minutes south. Medical care is available at HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, about twenty minutes west. The community's small scale creates a sense of privacy — this is not a master-planned development but a curated collection of homes sharing an exceptional ridgeline setting. Evening entertainment often means watching the sunset from a patio or the resort terrace, or making the twenty-seven-minute drive to Old Town Scottsdale's restaurant scene.

pois.name: The Grille at Eagle Mountain Golf Club

narrative.summary: Located in the Rio Verde Foothills. More affordable luxury entry point with outstanding golf and natural desert beauty.

body.lead: Tonto Verde is a two-course golf community in the Rio Verde foothills northeast of Fountain Hills, where the Sonoran Desert meets the Verde River drainage and the terrain opens to views of the Mazatzal Mountains, Four Peaks, and the Tonto National Forest. With homes from $400K to $1.5M and 36 holes of desert golf across the Ranch and Peaks courses, Tonto Verde offers a genuine retreat setting at a fraction of the cost of comparable North Scottsdale communities.

sections.content: Tonto Verde occupies the high desert terrain where Rio Verde transitions into the Tonto National Forest foothills. The landscape is classic upper Sonoran — saguaro, palo verde, and mesquite set against rocky hillsides and natural desert washes. The two golf courses — the Ranch Course and the Peaks Course — wind through this terrain with elevation changes and mountain views that rival the best courses in the Valley. Homes range from $400K patio homes to $1.5M custom estates on desert lots. The community sits at approximately 2,000 feet elevation, providing cooler evenings and clearer air than the valley floor. The Verde River corridor and Tonto National Forest are visible from many homesites.

Tonto Verde is a retreat community — the distance from the urban core is the point. The two courses offer 36 holes of uncrowded play. The clubhouse provides dining, a fitness center, pool, and social events. The surrounding desert and the nearby Tonto National Forest deliver hiking, horseback riding, and wildlife viewing. Fountain Hills' town center is twenty minutes south on McDowell Mountain Road for groceries, dining, and community events. Loop 101 is twenty-five minutes southwest, connecting to the Scottsdale corridor. Fashion Square is forty-seven minutes. Sky Harbor is forty-five minutes. The community attracts retirees, seasonal residents, and telecommuters who value the desert setting and golf lifestyle over urban proximity.

Daily life at Tonto Verde follows the rhythm of the desert. Early morning tee times, a hike on the community trails or into the Tonto National Forest, or a swim in the heated pool. The clubhouse handles meals and social connections. Groceries require a trip to Fountain Hills — Bashas' and Safeway are about twenty minutes south. The Rio Verde Country Store, closer by, handles basics. Medical care routes to HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, approximately thirty minutes southwest, or the Fountain Hills medical offices. The community' social calendar — member tournaments, holiday parties, card groups, and potluck dinners — fills the weeks. Evening entertainment is typically at home or at the clubhouse, where the sunset views over the desert and the star-filled skies are unmatched by anything closer to the city.

pois.name: Verde River Golf and Social Club

narrative.summary: Set in the Sonoran Desert foothills northeast of Scottsdale. Mountain views in every direction.

body.lead: Rio Verde Country Club is a private golf community in the far northeast Valley, set in the open desert terrain between Fountain Hills and the Verde River. With 36 holes across the Quail Run and White Wing courses, a full clubhouse, and homes from $350K to $1.5M, Rio Verde CC delivers private club living at a value that Scottsdale’s closer-in communities cannot match.

sections.content: Rio Verde Country Club spreads across the gently rolling desert terrain east of the McDowell Mountains, where the landscape transitions from the higher Sonoran Desert to the brushy grasslands of the Verde River drainage. Views extend to the Mazatzal Mountains, Four Peaks, and the distant Superstitions. The two courses — Quail Run and White Wing — are mature, well-conditioned layouts that take advantage of the natural terrain. Homes range from $350K attached villas and patio homes to $1.5M custom residences. The community has a distinctly relaxed, established character — many homes date from the 1970s through 1990s, with ongoing renovation and new construction filling in remaining lots.

Rio Verde Country Club operates as a fully private members-only facility. The clubhouse serves as the social center with formal and casual dining, a bar, fitness center, resort-style pool, tennis courts, and a full events calendar. The two courses provide 36 holes of uncrowded play for members. Fountain Hills' town center is seventeen minutes southwest for groceries, dining, and services. Loop 101 is twenty-three minutes for access to the Scottsdale corridor. Fashion Square is forty-five minutes. Sky Harbor is forty-three minutes. The community’s appeal is the combination of private club amenities at prices well below comparable communities closer to Scottsdale.

Mornings at Rio Verde CC start on the course or at the clubhouse fitness center. The club’s dining options handle breakfast and lunch without leaving the gates. Groceries require a trip to Fountain Hills — about seventeen minutes to Bashas' or Safeway. The Rio Verde general store covers basics. Medical care routes to HonorHealth Scottsdale Shea, approximately twenty-five minutes southwest, or the medical offices in Fountain Hills. Schools are in the Fountain Hills Unified District, though the community skews heavily toward retirees and seasonal residents. The social life centers on the club — member-guest tournaments, bridge groups, couples' dinners, and holiday celebrations. The quiet evenings and dark skies are a deliberate choice for residents who traded proximity for space, privacy, and value.

pois.name: Verde River Golf and Social Club

narrative.summary: High-desert landscape. Beautiful homes with unspoiled desert views.
