narrative.tagline: The View the Biltmore Looks Up To
narrative.summary: Prominent Biltmore area high-rise offering panoramic views and upscale urban living. Part of the corridor's growing luxury vertical residential scene.

body.lead: The Residences at 2211 Camelback is a boutique luxury condominium on the Camelback corridor in the Biltmore area, offering residences from $600K to $3M+ with proximity to Biltmore Fashion Park, the Esplanade, and the Valley's most established commercial district. The smaller building scale provides an intimate alternative to the corridor's larger high-rise developments.

sections.content: The Residences at 2211 Camelback occupies a prime position on Camelback Road in the Biltmore corridor, within walking distance of Biltmore Fashion Park and the Esplanade. The building is boutique in scale compared to the area's larger towers, offering a more intimate residential experience. Residences range from $600K to $3M+ with views of Camelback Mountain, Piestewa Peak, and the city. The building features pool, fitness, and common areas sized for its smaller resident community. The Camelback Road address places residents at the center of the corridor's office, retail, and dining concentration.

The 2211 Camelback lifestyle benefits from the same Biltmore corridor advantages as its larger neighbors — walkability to Fashion Park, proximity to SR-51, and the concentration of dining and retail along Camelback Road — but in a smaller, more personal building. Sky Harbor is nine minutes south. Fashion Square is thirteen minutes east. Biltmore Fashion Park is two minutes. The building's boutique scale means residents know their neighbors and the staff, creating a more residential feel than the larger towers. The Camelback corridor's medical offices, business centers, and services are all within a five-minute radius.

Daily life at 2211 Camelback follows the rhythm of the Biltmore corridor. Morning walks to the Fashion Park for coffee, a workout in the building's fitness center, or a quick drive to the Camelback Mountain Echo Canyon trailhead for a hike. Groceries at AJ's or Sprouts are within five minutes. The corridor's restaurant density means dining out requires no planning — Seasons 52, Zinburger, North Italia, and dozens of others are within walking or short driving distance. Medical care at the corridor's offices and Phoenix Children's Hospital is close. The building's smaller size means less bustle in the lobby and more personal service, attracting residents who want the location without the scale of a 200-unit tower.

narrative.summary: Luxury condos within Scottsdale's most exclusive community. Perfect for full-year residence or seasonal lock-and-leave.

body.lead: ICON at Silverleaf offers an ultra-luxury lock-and-leave lifestyle behind the guard gates of Scottsdale's most prestigious private community. These contemporary residences deliver full Silverleaf Club membership — golf, spa, dining, and social — in a maintenance-free format designed for buyers who split time between multiple homes.

sections.content: ICON sits within the Silverleaf community at DC Ranch, occupying elevated terrain along the eastern flanks of the McDowell Mountains. The location is distinctly private — accessed through Silverleaf's 24/7 guard-gated entry — yet positioned just minutes from the dining and shopping along the Pima Road corridor. Views from ICON residences sweep across the Sonoran Desert toward the McDowell Mountain Regional Park, with many units capturing both sunrise over the mountains and sunset over the valley. The community itself is compact and intentional, with desert landscaping, quiet streets, and an emphasis on architectural quality over density.

Silverleaf Club membership is the defining feature of life at ICON. The club's two Tom Weiskopf-designed championship courses rank among Arizona's finest private golf experiences. Beyond golf, the club campus includes a 25,000-square-foot spa and fitness center, multiple dining venues ranging from casual poolside to formal fine dining, resort-style pools, tennis and pickleball courts, and an active social calendar. ICON's lock-and-leave format — with exterior maintenance, landscaping, and security handled by the community — appeals to seasonal residents, frequent travelers, and executives who want a fully appointed Scottsdale address without the burden of estate maintenance. Architecture is contemporary with clean lines, premium materials, and expansive glass.

Despite its secluded feel, ICON benefits from DC Ranch's connectivity to the broader Scottsdale corridor. Market Street at DC Ranch — with boutique shopping, restaurants, and services — is minutes away within the community. Kierland Commons and Scottsdale Quarter are 15 minutes south, offering Nordstrom, Apple, and dozens of restaurants. Desert Ridge Marketplace is 14 minutes for major retail and entertainment. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 24 minutes via the 101 or Pima Road. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 20 minutes south via Thompson Peak Parkway. For outdoor recreation, the McDowell Sonoran Preserve trailheads — including Gateway and Tom's Thumb — are within 10 minutes. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 35 minutes.

narrative.summary: Strong sales trajectory. Belgravia Group's first Scottsdale project.

body.lead: Portico brings a contemporary vertical lifestyle to the heart of North Scottsdale's most connected corridor. Developed by Belgravia Group, these residences offer lock-and-leave convenience with resort-caliber amenities, placing residents minutes from Desert Ridge, Grayhawk, and the Loop 101 freeway.

sections.content: Portico occupies a central position along the Scottsdale Road / Hayden Road corridor near the intersection with the Loop 101, one of the most commercially vibrant stretches of North Scottsdale. The setting is distinctly urban by Scottsdale standards — closer to a walkable town center than a remote desert enclave. Views from upper residences capture the McDowell Mountains to the east and the broader Scottsdale landscape. The TPC Scottsdale course and Fairmont Scottsdale Princess are immediate neighbors, while the Musical Instrument Museum and Desert Ridge Marketplace anchor the retail and cultural scene just minutes north.

Portico's lifestyle centers on convenience without compromise. The community offers resort-style pools, fitness facilities, and curated common spaces designed for both socializing and quiet retreat. The lock-and-leave format handles exterior maintenance and landscaping, freeing residents from the typical homeowner burden. The Belgravia Group's design approach emphasizes open floor plans, high-end finishes, and floor-to-ceiling glass that maximizes natural light and views. Portico attracts a diverse buyer profile: downsizing professionals, seasonal residents, young luxury buyers, and anyone who values a sophisticated address with minimal maintenance.

Portico's location is arguably the most convenient of any luxury address in North Scottsdale. Desert Ridge Marketplace — with AMC, Dave & Buster's, H&M, Kona Grill, and dozens more — is just 9 minutes north. The Loop 101 is 10 minutes south, providing rapid access to the entire metro. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 21 minutes via the 101, and Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus matches at 21 minutes via Frank Lloyd Wright Boulevard. Whole Foods at Grayhawk and AJ's Fine Foods on Pima are both within 5 minutes for daily groceries. The Musical Instrument Museum, one of Phoenix's top cultural attractions, is under 10 minutes away. Grayhawk Golf Club and TPC Scottsdale are immediate neighbors for golf. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 30 minutes via the 101.

narrative.tagline: Named for a Legend. Built for the Next.
narrative.summary: Boutique-style condo living steps from Old Town's galleries, dining, and nightlife. Intimate scale with only 40 units.

body.lead: The Goldwater rises at the western edge of Paradise Valley where the Biltmore corridor meets the Phoenix Mountain Preserve, delivering luxury condominiums with mountain views and dual proximity to both the Biltmore and Scottsdale scenes. Named for Arizona's most famous political family, the development embodies the state's blend of Western heritage and modern ambition.

sections.content: The Goldwater occupies a distinctive position in western Paradise Valley, near the intersection of Lincoln Drive and Tatum Boulevard. This places it at a crossroads: the Biltmore corridor is 8 minutes south with Saks Fifth Avenue, The Capital Grille, and the Arizona Biltmore resort. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 11 minutes east via Lincoln Drive through the scenic heart of Paradise Valley. The Phoenix Mountain Preserve rises to the west, providing protected mountain views and hiking trail access. Piestewa Peak is visible from upper residences. The setting offers something unusual for a condo community: genuine desert surroundings and mountain views alongside urban connectivity.

The Goldwater caters to luxury buyers who want the lock-and-leave convenience of a condo with the views and setting usually reserved for estate homes. Rooftop amenities include a pool, lounge areas, and entertainment spaces with panoramic mountain and city-light views. Full-service concierge handles daily logistics, package delivery, and guest management. The residences feature contemporary finishes, open floor plans, and private terraces. The buyer profile includes downsizing luxury buyers from PV estates who want to stay in the area, professionals who work remotely and value the central position, and seasonal residents drawn to the Biltmore lifestyle without estate maintenance. The Hermosa Inn and LON's restaurant are a short drive. The Arizona Biltmore resort's golf, spa, and dining are minutes away.

The Goldwater's western PV position provides excellent balance between the Biltmore and Scottsdale corridors. Biltmore Fashion Park is 8 minutes south with upscale shopping and dining. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 11 minutes east via Lincoln Drive. AJ's Fine Foods is 5 minutes. The Arizona Biltmore resort is 5 minutes for golf, spa, and dining. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 16 minutes south via SR-51 — among the closest condo addresses to the airport. The SR-51 freeway entrance at Lincoln Drive provides quick connections to the entire metro. Piestewa Peak trailheads are accessible within 5 minutes. Whole Foods and Sprouts are within 10 minutes. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 26 minutes northeast.

narrative.summary: In the middle of Old Town's action. Walking distance to galleries, dining, shopping, and nightlife.

body.lead: Envy is a boutique luxury condominium in the walkable core of Old Town Scottsdale, offering an intimate alternative to the area's larger residential towers. At prices from $500K to $2M+, it delivers the full Old Town lifestyle — galleries, dining, nightlife, and Fashion Square — from a home base designed for buyers who value character and location over sheer scale.

sections.content: Envy sits in the heart of Old Town Scottsdale, east of Scottsdale Road near the intersection with 1st Avenue and Stetson Drive. The location places it in the walkable epicenter of Scottsdale's entertainment, arts, and dining district. Fashion Square is a 3-minute drive or short walk north. The Scottsdale Arts District, with its Thursday ArtWalk galleries, is steps away. The Scottsdale Civic Center, waterfront canal, and the entertainment district are all accessible on foot. As a boutique-scale development, Envy avoids the anonymous feeling of larger towers — residents know their neighbors, and the intimate scale creates a genuine sense of community.

Envy's lifestyle revolves around its Old Town location. The rooftop pool and terrace provide a social gathering point with views of Camelback Mountain and the Scottsdale skyline. But the real lifestyle draw is the neighborhood: walk to Citizen Public House for dinner, hit the Thursday ArtWalk galleries, browse Fashion Square, catch a show at the Scottsdale Center for the Performing Arts, or bar-hop through the entertainment district — all without moving your car. Residences feature modern finishes, open layouts, and private balconies. The buyer profile includes young professionals, remote workers, seasonal residents, and anyone who prioritizes walkable urban living over gated suburban estates.

Envy's Old Town position makes daily life refreshingly simple. Fashion Square and its luxury retail are 3 minutes by car, closer on foot. Dozens of restaurants are within walking distance. AJ's Fine Foods and Safeway are under 5 minutes. The Scottsdale Civic Center library and public spaces are a short walk. Biltmore Fashion Park is 14 minutes west. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 17 minutes south — excellent for frequent travelers. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn Medical Center is under 5 minutes. The Arizona Canal walking and cycling path is accessible nearby. Camelback Mountain trailheads are 10 minutes. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 24 minutes northeast via the 101.

narrative.summary: Elegant community within the iconic Arizona Biltmore Resort estate. Luxury living infused with the Biltmore's historic character.

body.lead: Two Biltmore Estates is a luxury condominium community within the grounds of the historic Arizona Biltmore Resort, offering residences from $600K to $3M+ in a setting defined by Frank Lloyd Wright-inspired architecture, resort golf, and the landmark property's landscaped grounds. The location provides resort-level living with the Biltmore corridor's urban conveniences three minutes south.

sections.content: Two Biltmore Estates sits within the Arizona Biltmore Resort property, surrounded by the resort's manicured grounds, golf courses, and the distinctive Frank Lloyd Wright-inspired architecture that defines one of Phoenix's most iconic addresses. The condominiums range from $600K to $3M+, with views of the resort's gardens, the golf fairways, Piestewa Peak, and Camelback Mountain. The setting is unique in the Valley — no other residential community is integrated into a historic resort property of this caliber. The Biltmore's recent renovation by Waldorf Astoria has upgraded the resort's facilities while maintaining the architectural heritage.

Two Biltmore Estates residents enjoy the resort's amenities as their extended backyard — the Biltmore Golf Club's two courses, the spa, multiple restaurants and bars, and the resort's event programming. Biltmore Fashion Park is three minutes south with Saks Fifth Avenue, Apple, and the corridor's restaurant row. SR-51 at Lincoln Drive is five minutes east, providing fast access to the Loop 101 and the Scottsdale corridor. Sky Harbor is ten minutes via SR-51. Fashion Square is twelve minutes east. The resort setting provides a buffer from the corridor's commercial activity — inside the gates, the pace is that of a destination resort.

Daily life at Two Biltmore Estates follows the resort rhythm. Morning walks through the Biltmore's gardens, a round on the Links or Adobe course, a spa treatment, or breakfast at Frank & Albert's. The resort handles entertainment, dining, and social connections. For daily errands, the Biltmore corridor's services are three minutes south — AJ's Fine Foods, the Sprouts on 24th Street, and the area's medical offices. Phoenix Children's Hospital is seven minutes. The community's HOA maintains the buildings and common areas to the standard expected of a resort-adjacent address. Evening options include the resort's bars and restaurants, the Fashion Park dining scene, or the short drive to Old Town Scottsdale.

pois.name: First United Methodist Church of Phoenix Weekday School

narrative.summary: Golf course-front luxury within the Biltmore estate. Combines condo convenience with resort living.

body.lead: Fairway Lodge at Biltmore Estates offers golf-frontage condominiums within the Arizona Biltmore Resort grounds, with residences from $500K to $2M+ overlooking the Biltmore Golf Club's fairways. The location combines the daily pleasure of living on a resort golf course with the Biltmore corridor's unmatched urban convenience — ten minutes from Sky Harbor and walkable to Phoenix's premier shopping district.

sections.content: Fairway Lodge sits along the fairways of the Biltmore Golf Club within the Arizona Biltmore Resort property. The condominiums offer direct golf course views — morning light on manicured fairways with Piestewa Peak and Camelback Mountain as the backdrop. Residences range from $500K to $2M+ in a variety of floor plans suited to the golf-lifestyle buyer. The Biltmore's Frank Lloyd Wright-inspired architecture and landscaped grounds provide the community's visual character. The resort's recent Waldorf Astoria renovation has elevated the property's amenities and dining while preserving its historic identity.

Fairway Lodge residents live on the golf course and within the resort ecosystem. The Biltmore Golf Club's two courses — the Links and the Adobe — are steps from the front door. The resort's spa, fitness facilities, restaurants, and bars serve as extended amenities. Biltmore Fashion Park is three minutes south. SR-51 at Lincoln Drive provides fast freeway access five minutes east. Sky Harbor is ten minutes. Fashion Square is twelve minutes. The lifestyle is defined by the combination of resort living and urban access — a pairing that is difficult to find elsewhere in the Valley at this price point.

Mornings at Fairway Lodge start with the golf course — watching early foursomes from the patio, or heading out for a round on the Links or Adobe course. The resort's dining options — Frank & Albert's, the bar, the pool cafe — handle meals throughout the day. For groceries and errands, the Biltmore corridor is three minutes south: AJ's Fine Foods, Sprouts, the post office, and the area's medical and dental offices. Phoenix Children's Hospital is seven minutes. The community attracts a mix of full-time residents, seasonal snowbirds, and occasional-use buyers who want a Valley pied-a-terre with resort amenities and airport proximity. The HOA fees reflect the resort-adjacent setting and the community's maintenance standards.

pois.name: Humanities and Sciences Academy of the United States

narrative.summary: Newer collection of luxury villas. Intimate scale.

body.lead: The Reserve is a boutique gated villa community in the upper Scottsdale corridor between Kierland and Desert Ridge. With residences from $1M to $3M+ in a low-density format, The Reserve caters to buyers seeking a refined, lock-and-leave lifestyle eight minutes from Desert Ridge Marketplace and twenty-four from Old Town Scottsdale.

sections.content: The Reserve occupies a discreet parcel in northeast Scottsdale, set back from the Scottsdale Road corridor between Desert Ridge and Pinnacle Peak. The community is intentionally low-density — a small collection of villa-style homes organized around manicured common areas with views of the McDowell Mountains and the Sonoran Preserve. Architecture is contemporary desert, with clean rooflines, stone accents, and indoor-outdoor living spaces. The gated entry and limited number of residences create an intimate, resort-like atmosphere that larger communities cannot match.

The Reserve appeals to a specific buyer: someone who wants North Scottsdale's desert setting and golf-corridor access without the sprawl of a master-planned community. Desert Ridge Marketplace is eight minutes south with Whole Foods, dining, and entertainment. Kierland Commons is twelve minutes. Fashion Square is twenty-four minutes via the 101. Sky Harbor is twenty-eight minutes. The Grayhawk and Troon golf courses are within fifteen minutes. The McDowell Sonoran Preserve trailheads are ten minutes east. Loop 101 access at Scottsdale Road or Pinnacle Peak provides efficient cross-Valley mobility.

Daily life at The Reserve follows the lock-and-leave model — the community's HOA handles exterior maintenance, landscaping, and common-area upkeep, freeing residents from yard work and desert cleanup. Morning walks through the community or a quick drive to the Sonoran Preserve trails set the day's pace. Groceries come from the Whole Foods at Desert Ridge or the AJ's at Tatum, both within ten minutes. The community's small size fosters genuine neighborly connections. Evening dining options include the Desert Ridge Marketplace restaurants, the Fairmont Princess, and the growing collection of chef-driven spots along the Frank Lloyd Wright Boulevard corridor. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is accessible within fifteen minutes.

pois.name: Gila River Indian Community Tribal Health
Eddie Merlot's Prime Aged Beef & Seafood

narrative.summary: Fully remodeled corner units commanding premium prices. Heart of Old Town walkability.

body.lead: The Mark Scottsdale is a premier luxury tower at the Scottsdale Road and Camelback corridor, delivering high-rise living with full-service amenities, concierge, and panoramic views from one of Old Town's most prominent addresses. With units from $800K to $3M+, The Mark targets buyers who want the vertical luxury experience with the substance to back it up.

sections.content: The Mark Scottsdale rises along the Scottsdale Road corridor near Camelback Road, commanding a skyline position in one of Old Town's most visible locations. The tower's height provides residents with views that rival hilltop estates: Camelback Mountain's red-rock face to the west, Mummy Mountain and Paradise Valley to the north, the McDowell Mountains to the northeast, and the Valley's city lights spreading south. Fashion Square is directly across the intersection. The Scottsdale Waterfront dining district is adjacent. The location is the ultimate urban-Scottsdale position — at the crossroads of everything the city offers.

The Mark delivers the full high-rise luxury experience: 24/7 concierge, valet parking, rooftop sky lounge with pool and entertaining spaces, state-of-the-art fitness center, private wine storage, and guest suites. The residences themselves feature floor-to-ceiling glass walls, designer kitchens, premium fixtures, and private terraces sized for outdoor dining. The lifestyle is social and connected — residents run into each other in the elevator lobby, meet for rooftop sunset cocktails, and share a sense of community that suburban estates rarely foster. The buyer profile includes corporate executives, seasonal residents maintaining a luxury pied-à-terre, professionals who work in the Scottsdale corridor, and downsizers from Paradise Valley estates who want to stay close to their former neighborhood.

The Mark's Scottsdale Road position provides the same exceptional walkability as its Old Town neighbors. Fashion Square is a 3-minute drive or easy walk. The Waterfront canal-side restaurants and shops are adjacent. Old Town's gallery and entertainment district is minutes south. Biltmore Fashion Park is 13 minutes west via Camelback Road. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 17 minutes south. AJ's Fine Foods is 5 minutes. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn Medical Center is under 5 minutes. The concierge handles restaurant reservations, car service, dry cleaning, and daily logistics — one of the advantages of tower living. Camelback Mountain trailheads are 10 minutes. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 24 minutes northeast.

narrative.tagline: Your First Act in Old Town
narrative.summary: Entry-level Old Town ownership. Popular with investors, second-home owners, and STR operators.

body.lead: Ciento delivers attainable luxury in south Old Town Scottsdale, offering modern condominiums at price points from $250K to $500K in a location that puts Fashion Square, Old Town dining, and Sky Harbor airport all within minutes. For first-time luxury buyers, investors, and seasonal residents seeking a Scottsdale address without the million-dollar entry, Ciento is a compelling starting point.

sections.content: Ciento sits in the southern reaches of Old Town Scottsdale, south of Camelback Road and east of Scottsdale Road, in a transitional neighborhood between Old Town's commercial energy and the quieter residential pockets near Papago Park. The setting is urban-adjacent: Scottsdale Road's dining and retail are steps away, the Arizona Canal path provides green space and walking routes, and Camelback Mountain is visible to the northwest. The community features a modern architectural aesthetic appropriate to its price point — clean lines, efficient floor plans, and communal amenities that maximize the value of each square foot.

Ciento's lifestyle is about access and value. At $250K–$500K, residents gain entry to the Scottsdale zip code and all it implies: walkable dining and entertainment, proximity to Fashion Square and Old Town, and a community pool and spa for daily recreation. The community attracts young professionals launching careers in the Scottsdale corridor, seasonal residents seeking an affordable winter base, and investors drawn to strong rental demand from the nearby entertainment district. The location south of Camelback Road means slightly lower prices than the core Old Town addresses, but the extra few minutes of driving is negligible. The Desert Botanical Garden and Papago Park are quick drives south for outdoor recreation.

Ciento's south Old Town position provides efficient access to the Valley's key destinations. Fashion Square and Old Town are 7 minutes north. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is just 12 minutes south — one of the shortest airport commutes from a Scottsdale address. The Desert Botanical Garden and Papago Park are 5 minutes south. Biltmore Fashion Park is 14 minutes west. AJ's Fine Foods, Safeway, and Sprouts are within 5 minutes. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn Medical Center is under 10 minutes. The Arizona Canal path provides walking and cycling access through central Scottsdale. ASU's Tempe campus is 15 minutes south. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is about 25 minutes northeast.

pois.name: Frank And Lupe's Restaurant and Bar

narrative.summary: Gated modern community in South Scottsdale. Popular with professionals seeking gated security and walkable lifestyle at a more accessible price point.

body.lead: Skye on McDowell occupies a strategic position in south Scottsdale where the city's urban fabric meets the natural landscape of Papago Park and the Desert Botanical Garden. At 11 minutes from Sky Harbor and 9 from Fashion Square, these modern condominiums offer a rare combination: urban Scottsdale living with immediate access to desert parkland and the Valley's fastest airport commute.

sections.content: Skye on McDowell sits along McDowell Road in south Scottsdale, positioned between Old Town to the north and Papago Park to the south. The Papago Buttes — Scottsdale's iconic red sandstone formations — are visible from the community, and the Desert Botanical Garden's 140-acre campus is a 3-minute drive. The setting is transitional: commercial McDowell Road provides services and connectivity, while the parkland to the south creates an unexpected sense of openness and nature. Views from upper residences capture Camelback Mountain to the northwest, the Papago Buttes to the south, and the McDowell Mountains to the northeast. The architecture is contemporary and clean, with the kind of modern aesthetic that appeals to design-conscious buyers.

Skye on McDowell attracts buyers who want to live between two worlds: Scottsdale's urban energy and the Sonoran Desert's natural beauty. The Desert Botanical Garden — one of the world's great desert gardens — is essentially a backyard amenity, offering morning walks among saguaros and evening events among luminarias. Papago Park's trails, fishing lagoons, and open desert are minutes away. Yet Old Town's restaurants, galleries, and nightlife are a quick 9-minute drive north. The community's amenities include a resort-style pool, fitness facilities, and communal spaces. The buyer profile includes outdoor enthusiasts, garden members, professionals who commute to the airport corridor, and seasonal residents who value nature proximity alongside urban access.

Skye on McDowell's south Scottsdale position provides some of the Valley's best airport access. Phoenix Sky Harbor is just 11 minutes south via McDowell Road or the 202 — ideal for business travelers and frequent flyers. Fashion Square and Old Town are 9 minutes north via Scottsdale Road. The Desert Botanical Garden is 3 minutes. Papago Park — with its trails, Phoenix Zoo, and Hole-in-the-Rock viewpoint — is adjacent. Tempe Town Lake and ASU's campus are 10 minutes south. Safeway, Sprouts, and local grocery options are within 5 minutes. Papago Golf Course is a neighbor. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn is under 10 minutes. The community's McDowell Road position also provides quick east-west connectivity across the Valley.

narrative.tagline: Minutes to Papago Park. Steps to Everything.
narrative.summary: Quiet single-family neighborhoods tucked behind McDowell and Scottsdale Roads. Many homes fully renovated with modern finishes.

body.lead: Papago Parkway and New Papago Parkway sit at Scottsdale's southern gateway, where the city meets Papago Park's 1,200 acres of desert parkland and the Papago Golf Course's championship fairways. At 10 minutes from both Sky Harbor and Fashion Square, these communities deliver the Valley's most balanced position — equidistant from the airport, Old Town, and Tempe's university district.

sections.content: Papago Parkway occupies the southern edge of Scottsdale, directly adjacent to Papago Park and the Papago Golf Course. The setting is remarkably green for the desert: the golf course's irrigated fairways, Papago Park's palo verde-lined trails, and the Desert Botanical Garden's cultivated landscapes create a corridor of vegetation that softens the urban grid. The iconic Papago Buttes — red sandstone formations rising from the desert floor — are a neighborhood landmark. Views from the community capture these buttes, Camelback Mountain to the northwest, and the McDowell Mountains to the northeast. The Phoenix Zoo borders the park. Tempe Town Lake and the Mill Avenue district are visible to the south.

Papago Parkway's lifestyle revolves around the extraordinary outdoor recreation at its doorstep. Papago Golf Course — a beloved municipal course designed by William F. Bell — is adjacent, offering affordable championship golf in a stunning desert setting. Papago Park provides miles of trails for hiking, mountain biking, and running. The Desert Botanical Garden hosts seasonal events, concerts, and art installations. The Phoenix Zoo is a family favorite. Hole-in-the-Rock provides a quick scramble with panoramic views. Yet the community is far from isolated — Old Town's dining scene is 10 minutes north, and Tempe's Mill Avenue and Town Lake entertainment district are 7 minutes south. Homes range from updated mid-century to newer construction, at price points that make this area one of Scottsdale's best values.

Papago Parkway's position at Scottsdale's southern tip creates a uniquely balanced commute profile. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is just 10 minutes — the closest from any Scottsdale community. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 10 minutes north. Tempe Town Lake, Mill Avenue, and ASU are 7 minutes south. The Desert Botanical Garden is 3 minutes. Papago Golf Course is next door. The 202 freeway provides quick east-west connections. Safeway, Fry's, and local markets are within 5 minutes. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn is under 10 minutes. The location is particularly attractive for professionals working in the Tempe-airport corridor, seasonal residents who want easy airport access, and families drawn to the park-adjacent lifestyle.

pois.name: Pho Fresh Alley and Boba Tea

narrative.summary: Classic South Scottsdale single-family neighborhood. Many homes fully renovated with contemporary finishes.

body.lead: Scottsdale Estates lines the Indian Bend Wash Greenbelt in south Scottsdale, offering a park-adjacent lifestyle that connects residents via walking and cycling paths from Eldorado Park north through the heart of the city. At 10 minutes from Fashion Square and 14 from Sky Harbor, these established neighborhoods deliver outdoor-oriented living at accessible price points.

sections.content: Scottsdale Estates occupies a series of established neighborhoods along the Indian Bend Wash Greenbelt in south-central Scottsdale, east of Hayden Road near Thomas Road. The Greenbelt — a linear park system that converted the former flood channel into miles of connected parks, lakes, paths, and recreation areas — is the community's defining feature. Eldorado Park, with its fishing lake, sports fields, and community pool, is adjacent. The setting is established suburban Scottsdale: mature trees, wide streets, and single-family homes with pools and citrus trees in the backyards. Camelback Mountain is visible to the northwest, and the commercial energy of Scottsdale Road is a short drive west.

Scottsdale Estates' lifestyle centers on the Greenbelt and its network of paths, parks, and recreation. Residents walk, jog, and cycle the Greenbelt from their front door, connecting to Eldorado Park's sports facilities, the Scottsdale Stadium spring training venue, and eventually to McCormick Ranch and north Scottsdale's path network. This is family-oriented living: good schools, safe streets, neighborhood parks, and a community feel that larger luxury developments sometimes lack. Homes range from classic 1970s-80s ranch-style residences to fully updated contemporary remodels, typically with pools and generous yards. The price point — $400K to $900K — makes this one of the most accessible addresses in the Scottsdale school district.

Scottsdale Estates' central-south position provides balanced access to the Valley's key destinations. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 10 minutes north via Hayden or Scottsdale Road. Tempe Town Lake and ASU are 8 minutes south. Phoenix Sky Harbor is 14 minutes southwest. Kierland Commons and Scottsdale Quarter are 15 minutes north. The Loop 101 freeway is accessible within 5 minutes at Thomas Road, connecting to the entire metro. Eldorado Park is walking distance. Fry's, Safeway, and Sprouts are within 5 minutes. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn is under 10 minutes. Scottsdale Stadium — spring training home of the San Francisco Giants — is a neighbor. The Greenbelt path system provides car-free commuting north through Scottsdale.

identity.name: Cavalier Vista
identity.city: South Scottsdale (south edge of Old Town)

narrative.summary: Hidden gem on Old Town's southern edge. Peaceful residential feel while being walking distance to Scottsdale's entertainment core.

body.lead: Cavalier Vista sits at the southern edge of Old Town Scottsdale, offering modern condominiums at $350K–$700K with walkable access to Old Town's dining and entertainment and quick proximity to Papago Park and Sky Harbor. For buyers seeking the Old Town lifestyle at accessible prices, Cavalier Vista delivers the Scottsdale address without the Scottsdale premium.

sections.content: Cavalier Vista occupies a position along the southern fringe of Old Town Scottsdale, south of Thomas Road near the Scottsdale Road corridor. The location bridges Old Town's walkable commercial energy to the north and the residential neighborhoods near Papago Park to the south. The setting is urban Scottsdale — Scottsdale Road's restaurants, shops, and services line the corridor in both directions. Camelback Mountain is visible to the northwest. The Papago Buttes and Desert Botanical Garden are a quick drive south. The architecture is contemporary, with the clean desert modern aesthetic that defines newer Scottsdale developments.

Cavalier Vista attracts buyers who want to live in the Old Town orbit without the high entry price of the core Scottsdale Road and Camelback addresses. The community is close enough to walk or bike to Old Town's restaurants, galleries, and entertainment — particularly the south end of the gallery district and the growing restaurant scene along Scottsdale Road south of Camelback. The lifestyle is social and connected: residents grab dinner at nearby restaurants, hit happy hour at Old Town spots, and enjoy the Desert Botanical Garden's seasonal events — all within minutes. Homes feature modern finishes, community pool and amenities, and the efficient floor plans that work well for single professionals, couples, and seasonal residents.

Cavalier Vista's south Old Town position provides excellent daily logistics. Fashion Square and Old Town's commercial core are 8 minutes north. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 12 minutes south — outstanding for a Scottsdale address. The Desert Botanical Garden and Papago Park are 5 minutes south. Biltmore Fashion Park is 15 minutes west. Safeway, AJ's Fine Foods, and local markets are within 5 minutes. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn Medical Center is under 10 minutes. Scottsdale Road provides continuous commercial services in both directions. The community's position south of Thomas Road means slightly lower home values than the core Old Town blocks, translating to better value for the same Scottsdale lifestyle.

narrative.summary: Luxury residences affiliated with the reimagined Mountain Shadows Resort. Modern design with dramatic Camelback Mountain backdrop.

body.lead: Mountain Shadows Residences are integrated with the reimagined Mountain Shadows resort, a mid-century modern landmark reborn at the base of Camelback Mountain's north face. The community pairs branded residential living with the resort's acclaimed amenities — including The Short Course par-3 golf, Hearth '61 restaurant, and resort pools — all set against one of the most iconic mountain backdrops in the Southwest.

sections.content: Mountain Shadows Residences sit at the base of Camelback Mountain's dramatic north face, where the mountain's distinctive red-rock ridgeline rises more than 1,000 feet above the resort campus. The original Mountain Shadows resort was a mid-century icon that hosted celebrities and presidents; the reimagined property preserves that legacy while bringing the architecture and amenities into the contemporary era. The residences are integrated into the resort campus, sharing its manicured grounds, desert landscaping, and mountain views. The setting is western Paradise Valley — close to the Biltmore corridor and the Phoenix Mountain Preserve — with Mummy Mountain visible to the north and the city lights of Phoenix stretching to the south and west.

Mountain Shadows offers a lifestyle that blends resort hospitality with residential permanence. The Short Course — an 18-hole par-3 layout designed by Forrest Richardson — provides a unique golf experience: challenging enough for serious players, approachable enough for social rounds, and dramatically beautiful with Camelback Mountain framing every hole. Hearth '61, the resort's signature restaurant, delivers farm-to-table Arizona cuisine that draws diners from across the Valley. The resort's pools, fitness center, and spa provide daily amenities without the overhead of a private country club. The architectural aesthetic is desert modern: clean lines, natural stone, and glass that maximizes the mountain views. Buyers include downsizing luxury residents, seasonal visitors, and professionals drawn to the lock-and-leave convenience of resort living.

Mountain Shadows' western Paradise Valley position provides balanced access to both Scottsdale and Phoenix. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 7 minutes east via Lincoln Drive and Invergordon. Biltmore Fashion Park is 10 minutes west — one of the shortest Biltmore commutes from any PV community. AJ's Fine Foods is 5 minutes away. The Borgata is a 5-minute drive for additional dining. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is 18 minutes south via the 44th Street corridor — among the closest PV addresses to the airport. The JW Marriott Camelback Inn is a neighbor. The Phoenician resort and Sanctuary on Camelback Mountain are under 10 minutes. Paradise Valley Country Club is minutes away. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 25 minutes northeast.

identity.name: Haven (DC Ranch Area)
identity.priceRange: TBD (custom pricing per sq ft — available Feb 2026)

narrative.summary: Within the DC Ranch ecosystem. Buyers can customize during reservation process.

body.lead: Haven represents the next generation of custom luxury in the DC Ranch foothills, offering buyers the rare opportunity to build from the ground up in one of North Scottsdale's most established luxury ecosystems. With custom pricing per square foot and anticipated 2026 delivery, Haven targets buyers who want a brand-new home with immediate access to DC Ranch's trail systems, Market Street, and community amenities.

sections.content: Haven occupies elevated terrain in the eastern reaches of the DC Ranch area, near the base of the McDowell Mountains where the developed corridor gives way to the McDowell Sonoran Preserve. The setting is noticeably more rugged and remote than the central DC Ranch neighborhoods — homesites back up to protected desert and preserve land, offering unobstructed mountain views and a genuine sense of solitude. Tom's Thumb Trail, one of Scottsdale's most popular hiking destinations, is essentially next door. The elevation provides sweeping views west across the Scottsdale valley and north toward the Four Peaks and Tonto National Forest.

Haven's lifestyle combines the freedom of custom building with the established infrastructure of the DC Ranch ecosystem. Buyers work with approved builders to design homes tailored to their specifications — from modern desert contemporary to transitional desert — on one- to two-acre homesites. DC Ranch's community amenities are accessible, including the Desert Camp Community Center, extensive trail network, and Market Street's curated mix of restaurants, boutiques, and professional services. For golf, both Silverleaf and the broader DC Ranch community offer world-class private options, while public courses at Grayhawk and We-Ko-Pa are within 20 minutes. The buyer profile here skews toward families and executives seeking a primary residence with room to build exactly what they envision.

Haven's eastern foothill location trades proximity for privacy. The drive to major commercial centers is longer than more central North Scottsdale addresses — Desert Ridge Marketplace and the Loop 101 are both about 29 minutes west via Happy Valley Road and Pinnacle Peak Road. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 38 minutes, and Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 37 minutes via Shea Boulevard. For daily needs, AJ's Fine Foods and the commercial centers along Pima Road and Thompson Peak are roughly 15 minutes. Market Street at DC Ranch offers neighborhood dining and shopping closer to home. The McDowell Sonoran Preserve provides world-class hiking and mountain biking literally at the doorstep. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 50 minutes south.

hoa.details: $200–$500/mo (Community Council + Ranch Association + neighborhood varies). Club at DC Ranch dues separate. 0.5% capital fee at closing. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

narrative.summary: Within a larger mixed-use hub including shopping, dining, and office spaces. Belgravia Group follow-up to successful Portico project.

body.lead: Atavia at One Scottsdale is part of the ambitious One Scottsdale mixed-use development at the corner of Pinnacle Peak and Scottsdale Roads. This community pairs luxury residences with planned retail, dining, and lifestyle amenities — creating a walkable urban village in the heart of North Scottsdale's most connected corridor.

sections.content: Atavia occupies a prominent position at Pinnacle Peak and Scottsdale Roads, one of North Scottsdale's busiest and most commercially active intersections. The One Scottsdale master plan envisions a cohesive mixed-use environment where residents can walk to restaurants, shops, fitness, and daily services without getting in a car — a rarity in the sprawling North Scottsdale landscape. The immediate surroundings include the TPC Scottsdale course to the south, the Fairmont Scottsdale Princess, and the Desert Ridge commercial corridor to the north. Views from upper residences capture the McDowell Mountains to the east and Pinnacle Peak to the north.

Atavia's lifestyle proposition is fundamentally different from the gated golf communities that dominate North Scottsdale. Here, the emphasis is on urban convenience and walkability within a luxury setting. The One Scottsdale development plan includes curated retail, chef-driven restaurants, wellness studios, and community gathering spaces at the doorstep. The residences themselves feature contemporary architecture with open floor plans, high-end finishes, and private outdoor spaces. The community appeals to a buyer who wants the energy of an urban neighborhood — morning coffee walks, impromptu dinners, weekend farmers markets — without sacrificing the quality and privacy expected at this price point.

Atavia's central North Scottsdale position makes daily logistics effortless. Desert Ridge Marketplace is 9 minutes north with major retail and entertainment. The Loop 101 freeway is 10 minutes south, providing fast connections to the entire metro. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 21 minutes via the 101 — excellent for North Scottsdale. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus matches at 21 minutes. Whole Foods at Grayhawk, AJ's Fine Foods, and Sprouts are all within 5–7 minutes. TPC Scottsdale and Grayhawk Golf Club are immediate neighbors. The Musical Instrument Museum is under 10 minutes. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 30 minutes via the 101.

narrative.summary: Billion-dollar project that could become one of Scottsdale's most iconic landmarks. Blends luxury residential with hotel, retail, and green space.

body.lead: The Parque is a new mixed-use luxury development rising on Scottsdale Road south of Camelback — the former CrackerJax entertainment site reborn as a walkable residential and retail destination. With residences from $600K to $3M+ and ground-floor retail, The Parque brings a new urban-luxury model to one of the Valley's most connected locations: five minutes from Fashion Square, fourteen from Sky Harbor.

sections.content: The Parque occupies a prime parcel on the east side of Scottsdale Road between Camelback and Indian School, directly on the corridor that connects Old Town Scottsdale to Paradise Valley. The development replaces the former CrackerJax amusement site with a contemporary mixed-use village featuring luxury residences, curated retail, dining, and public gathering spaces. Residences range from $600K condominiums and townhomes to $3M+ penthouse-level units, all with modern finishes, rooftop access, and views toward Camelback Mountain and Mummy Mountain.

The Parque is designed around walkability — ground-floor retail and restaurants eliminate the need to drive for everyday needs. Fashion Square is five minutes north on Scottsdale Road, with the entire Old Town dining and gallery scene within the same radius. Biltmore Fashion Park is ten minutes west. Sky Harbor is fourteen minutes south via the 101 and 202. Loop 101 access is three minutes at the Indian School interchange. The Phoenician resort, Camelback Mountain trailheads, and the Scottsdale waterfront are all within a ten-minute radius. This is a location for people who want Scottsdale's best address without the suburban drive.

Life at The Parque is designed to be car-optional for daily routines. Step downstairs for coffee and breakfast at ground-floor restaurants, walk to Fashion Square for shopping, or bike to the Scottsdale waterfront. When driving is needed, the Loop 101 interchange is three minutes away, connecting to the entire Valley. The Scottsdale Unified School District serves the area, with acclaimed charter and private school options nearby including Scottsdale Preparatory Academy. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn is five minutes for medical needs. Evening entertainment is at your doorstep — between The Parque's own retail and the surrounding Scottsdale Road dining corridor, options are limitless.

pois.name: The Bread And Honey House Arcadia
Frank And Lupe's Restaurant and Bar

narrative.summary: Billion-dollar project blending green architecture with desert-modern living. One of the most eco-forward residential communities in Arizona.

body.lead: Optima McDowell Mountain Village is a billion-dollar green architecture project that brings eco-forward luxury living to the heart of Scottsdale. With 16 acres of rooftop gardens, multiple resort-style amenity decks, and a location just 10 minutes from Fashion Square, this development redefines what luxury vertical living looks like in the Valley.

sections.content: Optima McDowell Mountain Village occupies a substantial campus in central Scottsdale near the intersection of Hayden Road and McDowell Road, placing it squarely between Old Town Scottsdale and the Papago Park corridor. Despite the "McDowell Mountain" name, this is a central Scottsdale location — not the far-north McDowell Mountain communities. The setting is urban and connected, with Scottsdale's arts district, gallery scene, and restaurant row within walking or short driving distance. The development's signature feature is its architecture: terraced buildings with extensive rooftop gardens that create a green oasis in the desert urban fabric. Views capture Camelback Mountain, the McDowell Mountains, and the downtown Scottsdale skyline.

Optima's lifestyle centers on its extraordinary amenity campus. Sixteen acres of rooftop gardens — among the largest in the nation — provide lush outdoor spaces for walking, socializing, and quiet retreat. Multiple resort-style pools, a full fitness center, yoga and Pilates studios, sports courts, and community event spaces create a resort-within-a-residence experience. The architecture emphasizes sustainability with passive solar design, drought-tolerant landscaping, and energy-efficient systems. Units range from studios to expansive penthouses, with floor-to-ceiling glass, modern kitchens, and private terraces. The buyer profile includes young professionals, downsizing luxury buyers, seasonal residents, and anyone drawn to the intersection of sustainability and sophistication.

Optima's central location delivers the shortest commute times of almost any luxury community in the Scottsdale market. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are just 10 minutes north — walkable for ambitious pedestrians, a quick Uber for everyone else. HonorHealth Scottsdale Osborn Medical Center is less than 5 minutes away for immediate medical needs, with Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus 22 minutes north via the 101. Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport is just 11 minutes via the 202 freeway — remarkably close for a Scottsdale address. The Loop 101 is accessible in 17 minutes for connections north. Scottsdale's restaurant scene on Camelback Road, Scottsdale Road, and in Old Town is essentially at the doorstep. Papago Park and the Desert Botanical Garden are minutes south.

pois.name: Frank And Lupe's Restaurant and Bar

narrative.summary: Luxury condo entry point within the Desert Ridge master plan. Resort-style amenities with golf course views as standard.

body.lead: The Luxe at Desert Ridge represents the newest generation of luxury living in North Phoenix's most established master-planned corridor. Positioned adjacent to the JW Marriott Resort and Desert Ridge Marketplace, these semi-custom homes offer contemporary architecture, modern floorplans, and immediate access to the lifestyle infrastructure that makes Desert Ridge the north Valley's most connected community.

sections.content: The Luxe occupies a prime infill parcel within the Desert Ridge master plan, adjacent to the JW Marriott Resort & Spa. Homes range from $500K to $1.5M+ with contemporary desert architecture — clean rooflines, floor-to-ceiling glass, and indoor-outdoor living designed for the climate. The community benefits from Desert Ridge's mature landscape and established infrastructure while offering the latest in energy efficiency, smart-home integration, and open-concept design. Views extend east toward the McDowell Mountains and north across the Sonoran Preserve.

The Luxe inherits all of Desert Ridge's lifestyle advantages with the polish of new construction. Desert Ridge Marketplace is a two-minute drive — one of the Valley's largest open-air destinations with Whole Foods, major dining, and entertainment. The JW Marriott's two Wildfire courses, spa, and resort pools are essentially next door. Loop 101 and SR-51 are three minutes away, providing twenty-one-minute access to Sky Harbor and twenty-two minutes to Old Town Scottsdale. The Reach 11 trail system and Sonoran Desert preserve lands are within cycling distance for outdoor recreation.

Daily routines at The Luxe benefit from Desert Ridge's maturity as a community — the Marketplace serves as the daily errand hub, with Whole Foods for groceries, multiple coffee shops for morning meetings, and a full-service AMC for weekend entertainment. Schools in Cave Creek Unified and Paradise Valley Unified districts serve the area, with Desert Trails Elementary and Pinnacle High among the top-rated campuses. Morning fitness might be a run on the Reach 11 trails or a class at one of the Marketplace's boutique studios. HonorHealth Deer Valley is ten minutes west, and the JW Marriott's restaurants offer an easy solution for evenings when no one wants to cook.

hoa.details: $100–$300/mo (varies by sub-community). Wildfire Golf Club dues separate. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*

narrative.summary: Becoming a cornerstone of North Scottsdale's Loop 101 corridor. Mixed-use vision with significant residential expansion planned.

body.lead: Cavasson Expansion extends the successful Cavasson brand into a new phase along the Loop 101 corridor in North Scottsdale. This mixed-use residential and commercial development builds on the original Cavasson community's reputation for contemporary design and Grayhawk-adjacent convenience, with additional amenities and services planned on-site.

sections.content: The Cavasson Expansion sits along the Pima Road / Loop 101 corridor in the Grayhawk area of North Scottsdale, slightly north and east of the original Cavasson community. The location takes advantage of one of Scottsdale's most commercially active stretches — Whole Foods, AJ's Fine Foods, and numerous restaurants are within minutes along Pima Road. The Grayhawk Golf Club is visible to the north, and the Pinnacle Peak Vistas and McDowell Mountains provide a dramatic backdrop to the east. The development's master plan includes both residential and commercial components, aiming to create a more self-contained neighborhood than a typical single-use residential enclave.

As an expansion of the Cavasson brand, this community inherits the design philosophy of contemporary desert architecture with clean lines, premium materials, and indoor-outdoor flow. The mixed-use component adds a dimension that the original Cavasson lacks — on-site dining, retail, and services that reduce the need to leave the neighborhood for daily needs. Proximity to Grayhawk Golf Club provides easy access to the Raptor and Talon courses, while the TPC Scottsdale and Fairmont Scottsdale Princess are a short drive south. The community targets buyers who value the Grayhawk corridor's combination of golf, dining, and freeway access but want a newer product with additional walkable amenities.

The Cavasson Expansion's Grayhawk-area location puts daily conveniences close at hand. Desert Ridge Marketplace is 10 minutes north for major retail and entertainment. The Loop 101 freeway is approximately 12 minutes south, connecting to Kierland Commons, Scottsdale Quarter, and the broader metro. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 21 minutes via the 101. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is also 21 minutes via Frank Lloyd Wright Boulevard. Whole Foods and AJ's Fine Foods are within 5 minutes on Pima Road. The Musical Instrument Museum is under 10 minutes. Grayhawk and TPC Scottsdale are immediate neighbors for golf. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 32 minutes via the 101.

pois.name: Eddie Merlot's Prime Aged Beef & Seafood

narrative.tagline: Arizona's First Net Zero Luxury Address
narrative.summary: Appeals to luxury buyers prioritizing sustainability. Represents the growing "green luxury" segment in Scottsdale.

body.lead: Paloma is pioneering the growing "green luxury" segment in North Scottsdale, proving that sustainability and sophistication are not mutually exclusive. This development brings solar-standard, energy-efficient homes to the desert highlands between the Troon corridor and the McDowell Mountains, targeting buyers who want their home to reflect their environmental values without compromising on design or finish quality.

sections.content: Paloma occupies elevated terrain in the desert highlands between the established Troon and DC Ranch corridors of North Scottsdale. The setting is quintessential high desert — mature saguaro cacti, rocky washes, and panoramic mountain views in every direction. The McDowell Mountains rise to the east, Pinnacle Peak is visible to the northwest, and the Scottsdale valley spreads south toward Camelback Mountain. The community is far enough north to feel genuinely removed from the urban grid, yet connected enough via Pima Road and the 101 corridor to avoid the isolation of the far-north enclaves. Native desert vegetation is preserved throughout the community, with architectural guidelines that mandate earth-tone palettes and low-profile design.

Paloma's defining feature is its commitment to sustainable luxury. Every home comes solar-equipped as standard, with energy-efficient HVAC systems, high-performance windows, and EV-ready garages. The community targets net-zero energy performance — unusual in the luxury desert market. But sustainability doesn't mean sacrifice: homes feature the same open floor plans, chef's kitchens, and indoor-outdoor living spaces expected at this price point. Natural materials — stone, wood, weathered steel — complement the desert setting. The outdoor-oriented lifestyle includes trail access to the McDowell Sonoran Preserve, community walking paths, and homes designed to maximize shade and cross-ventilation. Buyers tend to be environmentally conscious professionals, tech executives, and families who see green building as the future of luxury.

Paloma's highland location means slightly longer drives to commercial centers, balanced by the privacy and views the setting provides. Desert Ridge Marketplace is 18 minutes west for major retail and entertainment. The Loop 101 freeway is 19 minutes south via Pima Road. Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 27 minutes for dining and nightlife. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is also 27 minutes south. AJ's Fine Foods and the commercial centers along Pima Road near Happy Valley are about 10 minutes for daily groceries. For golf, Troon North and Grayhawk are both within 15 minutes. The McDowell Sonoran Preserve offers world-class hiking and mountain biking nearby. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 40 minutes via the 101.

narrative.summary: Led by tech billionaire. Promises to reshape North Scottsdale's residential and commercial landscape.

body.lead: The CrowdStrike CEO Development is a landmark project led by tech billionaire George Kurtz that promises to reshape the North Scottsdale luxury landscape. Situated on limited remaining land in the coveted corridor between Troon and the McDowell Mountains, this development is expected to set new standards for ultra-luxury residential design in the Valley.

sections.content: The Kurtz development occupies one of the last significant undeveloped parcels in the premium North Scottsdale corridor, positioned between the established Troon community to the north and the DC Ranch / Pinnacle Peak area to the south. The site sits at roughly 2,200 feet elevation on gently rising terrain with commanding views of Pinnacle Peak, the McDowell Mountains, and the Scottsdale valley below. The scarcity of remaining developable land at this latitude — most surrounding acreage is either built out or protected preserve — makes this project inherently significant. The setting combines the rugged beauty of the high Sonoran Desert with proximity to the commercial and lifestyle amenities along the Pima Road corridor.

Details of the Kurtz development are still emerging, but the project is expected to target the ultra-luxury segment with a technology-forward approach reflecting the developer's background. Early indications suggest smart-home integration, sustainable building practices, and amenity programming that goes beyond the traditional golf-and-clubhouse model. The limited land availability means a smaller, more exclusive community footprint — likely fewer homes at higher price points. The location provides access to Troon North Golf Club, the McDowell Sonoran Preserve trail network, and the dining and cultural scene along the Scottsdale corridor. The buyer profile will likely include tech executives, entrepreneurs, and luxury buyers drawn to the prestige of a development backed by one of Arizona's most prominent business figures.

The development's North Scottsdale location places it in the same convenience tier as Troon and the upper DC Ranch area. Desert Ridge Marketplace is 20 minutes west for major retail, dining, and entertainment. The Loop 101 freeway is 20 minutes south via Pima Road, connecting to Kierland, Scottsdale Quarter, and the broader metro freeway system. Mayo Clinic's Scottsdale campus is 28 minutes south, and Fashion Square and Old Town Scottsdale are 29 minutes. AJ's Fine Foods and the commercial centers along Pima Road at Happy Valley are approximately 10 minutes for daily needs. Troon North and Grayhawk golf courses are both within 15 minutes. The McDowell Sonoran Preserve trailheads are nearby for hiking and biking. Phoenix Sky Harbor is approximately 40 minutes via the 101.

hoa.details: TBD — Ultra-luxury new development; HOA structure not yet established. *Verify current rates with HOA/management company before purchase.*
